correct.C: Correct. appspec.yml/appspec.yaml defines CodeDeploy deployment hooks and resources, including Lambda/serverless deployments. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codedeploy/latest/userguide/reference-appspec-file.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. dockerrun.aws.json is for Elastic Beanstalk Docker environments, not CodeDeploy serverless deployments.
wrong.B: Incorrect. buildspec.yml defines CodeBuild build commands, not CodeDeploy deployment instructions.
wrong.D: Incorrect. ebextensions.config is for Elastic Beanstalk environment customization.

correct.C: Correta. appspec.yml/appspec.yaml define hooks e recursos do CodeDeploy, inclusive deployments Lambda/serverless. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codedeploy/latest/userguide/reference-appspec-file.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. dockerrun.aws.json é para ambientes Docker do Elastic Beanstalk, não deployments serverless no CodeDeploy.
wrong.B: Incorreta. buildspec.yml define comandos do CodeBuild, não instruções do CodeDeploy.
wrong.D: Incorreta. ebextensions.config é para customização de ambiente Elastic Beanstalk.

question.en: A Developer is working on an application that handles 10MB documents that contain highly-sensitive data. The application will use AWS KMS to perform clientside encryption. What steps must be followed?
question.pt: Um Developer está trabalhando em uma aplicação que manipula documentos de 10 MB com dados altamente sensíveis. A aplicação usará AWS KMS para client-side encryption. Quais etapas devem ser seguidas?

en: Invoke the Encrypt API passing the plaintext data that must be encrypted, then reference the customer managed key ARN in the `KeyId` parameter.
Invoke the `GenerateRandom` API to get a data encryption key, then use the data encryption key to encrypt the data.
Invoke the `GenerateDataKey` API to retrieve the encrypted version of the data encryption key to encrypt the data.
Invoke the `GenerateDataKey` API to retrieve the plaintext version of the data encryption key to encrypt the data.
pt: Invocar a API `Encrypt` passando os dados em plaintext que devem ser criptografados e referenciar o ARN da customer managed key no parâmetro `KeyId`.
Invocar a API `GenerateRandom` para obter uma data encryption key e usá-la para criptografar os dados.
Invocar a API `GenerateDataKey` para recuperar a versão criptografada da data encryption key para criptografar os dados.
Invocar a API `GenerateDataKey` para recuperar a versão em plaintext da data encryption key para criptografar os dados.

correct.D: Correct. GenerateDataKey returns a plaintext data key for local encryption and an encrypted copy for later decryption, which is envelope encryption. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/concepts.html#enveloping

wrong.A: Incorrect. KMS Encrypt has a small plaintext size limit and is not used to encrypt 10 MB directly.
wrong.B: Incorrect. GenerateRandom returns random bytes, not a KMS-protected data key pair suitable for envelope encryption.
wrong.C: Incorrect. The encrypted data key is stored with the ciphertext but cannot encrypt the document directly because it is encrypted.

correct.D: Correta. GenerateDataKey retorna uma data key em plaintext para criptografia local e uma cópia criptografada para descriptografia futura, padrão de envelope encryption. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/concepts.html#enveloping

wrong.A: Incorreta. KMS Encrypt tem limite pequeno de plaintext e não deve criptografar 10 MB diretamente.
wrong.B: Incorreta. GenerateRandom retorna bytes aleatórios, não um par de data key protegido pelo KMS para envelope encryption.
wrong.C: Incorreta. A data key criptografada é armazenada com o ciphertext, mas não criptografa o documento diretamente porque está criptografada.

question.en: A Developer is building a web application that uses Amazon API Gateway to expose an AWS Lambda function to process requests from clients. During testing, the Developer notices that the API Gateway times out even though the Lambda function finishes under the set time limit. Which of the following API Gateway metrics in Amazon CloudWatch can help the Developer troubleshoot the issue? (Choose TWO)
question.pt: Um Developer está construindo uma aplicação web que usa Amazon API Gateway para expor uma função AWS Lambda que processa requisições de clientes. Durante os testes, o Developer percebe que o API Gateway atinge timeout mesmo que a função Lambda finalize dentro do limite configurado. Quais métricas do API Gateway no Amazon CloudWatch podem ajudar a investigar o problema? (Escolha DUAS)

en: CacheHitCount.
IntegrationLatency.
CacheMissCount.
Latency.
Count.
pt: CacheHitCount.
IntegrationLatency.
CacheMissCount.
Latency.
Count.

correct.B: Correct. IntegrationLatency measures time spent between API Gateway and the backend integration, helping isolate Lambda/integration delay. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-function-common.html
correct.D: Correct. Latency measures total API Gateway request latency, useful to compare against IntegrationLatency. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-function-common.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. CacheHitCount only indicates API Gateway cache hits and does not diagnose integration timeout latency.
wrong.C: Incorrect. CacheMissCount is about API cache behavior, not why requests time out.
wrong.E: Incorrect. Count shows request volume, not where timeout time is spent.

correct.B: Correta. IntegrationLatency mede o tempo entre API Gateway e integração backend, ajudando a isolar atraso na integração Lambda. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-function-common.html#configuration-function-folders
correct.D: Correta. Latency mede latência total da requisição no API Gateway, útil para comparar com IntegrationLatency. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-function-common.html#configuration-function-folders

wrong.A: Incorreta. CacheHitCount só indica acertos de cache do API Gateway e não diagnostica latência de integração.
wrong.C: Incorreta. CacheMissCount trata comportamento de cache da API, não causa de timeout.
wrong.E: Incorreta. Count mostra volume de requisições, não onde o tempo do timeout é gasto.

question.en: A company needs to distribute firmware updates to its customers around the world. Which service will allow easy and secure control of the access to the downloads at the lowest cost?
question.pt: Uma empresa precisa distribuir atualizações de firmware para clientes ao redor do mundo. Qual serviço permite controle fácil e seguro do acesso aos downloads pelo menor custo?

en: Use Amazon CloudFront with signed URLs for Amazon S3.
Create a dedicated Amazon CloudFront Distribution for each customer.
Use Amazon CloudFront with AWS Lambda@Edge.
Use Amazon API Gateway and AWS Lambda to control access to an S3 bucket.
pt: Usar Amazon CloudFront com signed URLs para Amazon S3.
Criar uma distribuição CloudFront dedicada para cada cliente.
Usar Amazon CloudFront com AWS Lambda@Edge.
Usar Amazon API Gateway e AWS Lambda para controlar acesso a um bucket S3.

correct.A: Correct. CloudFront signed URLs in front of S3 provide global distribution with controlled time-limited access at low cost. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/PrivateContent.html

wrong.B: Incorrect. One distribution per customer increases operational overhead and cost unnecessarily.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Lambda@Edge can customize logic, but is unnecessary and more expensive for simple signed download access.
wrong.D: Incorrect. API Gateway plus Lambda adds compute and request cost for a use case CloudFront signed URLs handles natively.

correct.A: Correta. CloudFront signed URLs na frente do S3 fornecem distribuição global com acesso controlado e temporário a baixo custo. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudFront/latest/DeveloperGuide/PrivateContent.html

wrong.B: Incorreta. Uma distribuição por cliente aumenta custo e overhead sem necessidade.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Lambda@Edge pode customizar lógica, mas é desnecessário e mais caro para downloads assinados simples.
wrong.D: Incorreta. API Gateway com Lambda adiciona custo de computação/requisição para um caso que CloudFront signed URLs resolve nativamente.

question.en: An application writes items to an Amazon DynamoDB table. As the application scales to thousands of instances, calls to the DynamoDB API generate occasional `ThrottlingException` errors. The application is coded in a language incompatible with the AWS SDK. How should the error be handled?
question.pt: Uma aplicação grava itens em uma tabela Amazon DynamoDB. À medida que a aplicação escala para milhares de instâncias, chamadas para a API do DynamoDB geram erros ocasionais de `ThrottlingException`. A aplicação foi escrita em uma linguagem incompatível com o AWS SDK. Como o erro deve ser tratado?

en: Add exponential backoff to the application logic.
Use Amazon SQS as an API message bus.
Pass API calls through Amazon API Gateway.
Send the items to DynamoDB through Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose.
pt: Adicionar exponential backoff à lógica da aplicação.
Usar Amazon SQS como barramento de mensagens da API.
Passar as chamadas de API pelo Amazon API Gateway.
Enviar os itens para o DynamoDB por meio do Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose.

correct.A: Correct. Exponential backoff is the correct client-side retry strategy for throttling, especially when SDK automatic retries are unavailable. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/Programming.Errors.html

wrong.B: Incorrect. SQS can buffer writes but changes architecture and does not directly handle DynamoDB API throttling in the caller.
wrong.C: Incorrect. API Gateway does not remove DynamoDB throttling and may add another throttle layer.
wrong.D: Incorrect. Kinesis Data Firehose is not the general write API for arbitrary DynamoDB item writes.

correct.A: Correta. Exponential backoff é a estratégia correta de retry para throttling, especialmente sem retries automáticos do SDK. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/Programming.Errors.html

wrong.B: Incorreta. SQS pode bufferizar gravações, mas muda a arquitetura e não trata diretamente throttling da API no chamador.
wrong.C: Incorreta. API Gateway não remove throttling do DynamoDB e pode adicionar outro limite.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Kinesis Data Firehose não é a API geral para gravar itens arbitrários no DynamoDB.

question.en: An e-commerce web application that shares session state on-premises is being migrated to AWS. The application must be fault tolerant, natively highly scalable, and any service interruption should not affect the user experience. What is the best option to store the session state?
question.pt: Uma aplicação web de e-commerce que compartilha estado de sessão on-premises está sendo migrada para a AWS. A aplicação deve ser tolerante a falhas, altamente escalável de forma nativa, e qualquer interrupção de serviço não deve afetar a experiência do usuário. Qual é a melhor opção para armazenar o estado da sessão?

en: Store the session state in Amazon ElastiCache.
Store the session state in Amazon CloudFront.
Store the session state in Amazon S3.
Enable session stickiness using elastic load balancers.
pt: Armazenar o estado da sessão no Amazon ElastiCache.
Armazenar o estado da sessão no Amazon CloudFront.
Armazenar o estado da sessão no Amazon S3.
Habilitar session stickiness usando Elastic Load Balancers.

correct.A: Correct. ElastiCache is a low-latency, scalable shared session store; Redis replication/Multi-AZ can improve fault tolerance. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/sticky-sessions.html

wrong.B: Incorrect. CloudFront caches content at edge locations; it is not a mutable session state database.
wrong.C: Incorrect. S3 is durable but object-storage latency is not ideal for active session state.
wrong.D: Incorrect. Sticky sessions keep state on one server and fail when that server is interrupted, so they do not meet fault tolerance.

correct.A: Correta. ElastiCache é armazenamento compartilhado de sessão escalável e de baixa latência; Redis com replicação/Multi-AZ aumenta tolerância a falhas. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/sticky-sessions.html

wrong.B: Incorreta. CloudFront faz cache de conteúdo na borda; não é banco mutável de estado de sessão.
wrong.C: Incorreta. S3 é durável, mas a latência de object storage não é ideal para sessões ativas.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Sticky sessions mantêm estado em um servidor e falham quando ele cai, não atendendo tolerância a falhas.

question.en: A Developer is creating a template that uses AWS CloudFormation to deploy an application. This application is serverless and uses Amazon API Gateway, Amazon DynamoDB, and AWS Lambda. Which tool should the Developer use to define simplified syntax for expressing serverless resources?
question.pt: Um Developer está criando um template que usa AWS CloudFormation para implantar uma aplicação. A aplicação é serverless e usa Amazon API Gateway, Amazon DynamoDB e AWS Lambda. Qual ferramenta o Developer deve usar para definir uma sintaxe simplificada para expressar recursos serverless?

en: CloudFormation serverless intrinsic functions.
AWS serverless express.
An AWS serverless application model.
A CloudFormation serverless plugin.
pt: CloudFormation serverless intrinsic functions.
AWS serverless express.
AWS Serverless Application Model.
CloudFormation serverless plugin.

correct.C: Correct. AWS SAM provides simplified syntax for API Gateway, Lambda, DynamoDB-related serverless applications and transforms into CloudFormation. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/serverless-application-model/latest/developerguide/sam-specification.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. CloudFormation intrinsic functions are not the serverless simplified syntax model.
wrong.B: Incorrect. AWS Serverless Express is a framework/library pattern, not the CloudFormation transform for serverless resources.
wrong.D: Incorrect. A generic “serverless plugin” is not the AWS-native CloudFormation tool.

correct.C: Correta. AWS SAM fornece sintaxe simplificada para aplicações serverless com API Gateway, Lambda e DynamoDB, transformando em CloudFormation. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/serverless-application-model/latest/developerguide/sam-specification.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Intrinsic functions do CloudFormation não são o modelo de sintaxe simplificada serverless.
wrong.B: Incorreta. AWS Serverless Express é framework/biblioteca, não o transform do CloudFormation para recursos serverless.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Um “serverless plugin” genérico não é a ferramenta nativa do CloudFormation na AWS.

question.en: A Developer has a stateful web server on-premises that is being migrated to AWS. The Developer must have greater elasticity in the new design. How should the Developer re-factor the application to make it more elastic? (Choose TWO)
question.pt: Um Developer possui um web server stateful on-premises que será migrado para a AWS. O Developer precisa de maior elasticidade no novo desenho. Como a aplicação deve ser refatorada para se tornar mais elástica? (Escolha DUAS)

en: Use pessimistic concurrency on Amazon DynamoDB.
Use Amazon CloudFront with an Auto Scaling group.
Use Amazon CloudFront with an AWS Web Application Firewall.
Store session state data in an Amazon DynamoDB table.
Use an ELB with an Auto Scaling group.
pt: Usar concorrência pessimista no Amazon DynamoDB.
Usar Amazon CloudFront com um Auto Scaling group.
Usar Amazon CloudFront com AWS Web Application Firewall.
Armazenar dados de estado de sessão em uma tabela Amazon DynamoDB.
Usar um ELB com um Auto Scaling group.

correct.D: Correct. Moving session state to DynamoDB removes server affinity and allows stateless web servers to scale out/in. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/sticky-sessions.html
correct.E: Correct. ELB with Auto Scaling distributes traffic across a variable number of stateless servers. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/sticky-sessions.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Pessimistic concurrency reduces elasticity by introducing locking and contention.
wrong.B: Incorrect. CloudFront can improve content delivery, but Auto Scaling for the web tier needs load balancing and session externalization.
wrong.C: Incorrect. WAF protects against web attacks; it does not make the application state scalable.

correct.D: Correta. Mover sessão para DynamoDB remove afinidade com servidor e permite web servers stateless escalarem. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/Introduction.html
correct.E: Correta. ELB com Auto Scaling distribui tráfego entre número variável de servidores stateless. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/Introduction.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Concorrência pessimista reduz elasticidade ao introduzir locks e contenção.
wrong.B: Incorreta. CloudFront melhora entrega de conteúdo, mas a camada web precisa de balanceamento e externalização de sessão.
wrong.C: Incorreta. WAF protege contra ataques web; não torna o estado da aplicação escalável.

question.en: A Developer must analyze performance issues with production-distributed applications written as AWS Lambda functions. These distributed Lambda applications invoke other components that make up the applications. How should the Developer identify and troubleshoot the root cause of the performance issues in production?
question.pt: Um Developer precisa analisar problemas de performance em aplicações distribuídas em produção escritas como funções AWS Lambda. Essas aplicações Lambda distribuídas invocam outros componentes que fazem parte das aplicações. Como o Developer deve identificar e solucionar a causa raiz dos problemas de performance em produção?

en: Add logging statements to the Lambda functions, then use Amazon CloudWatch to view the logs.
Use AWS Cloud Trail and then examine the logs.
Use AWS X-Ray, then examine the segments and errors.
Run Amazon Inspector agents and then analyze performance.
pt: Adicionar instruções de logging às funções Lambda e usar Amazon CloudWatch para visualizar os logs.
Usar AWS CloudTrail e depois examinar os logs.
Usar AWS X-Ray e examinar os segments e erros.
Executar agentes do Amazon Inspector e analisar performance.

correct.C: Correct. X-Ray traces distributed requests, segments, subsegments, errors, and latency across Lambda-based applications. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/xray/latest/devguide/xray-services-lambda.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. CloudWatch logs help with messages and errors, but do not provide distributed traces across Lambda and downstream components.
wrong.B: Incorrect. CloudTrail audits AWS API calls; it is not an application performance tracing tool.
wrong.D: Incorrect. Inspector assesses security/vulnerabilities on supported workloads, not Lambda performance traces.

correct.C: Correta. X-Ray rastreia requisições distribuídas, segments, subsegments, erros e latência em aplicações Lambda. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/xray/latest/devguide/xray-services-lambda.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. CloudWatch Logs ajuda com mensagens e erros, mas não fornece traces distribuídos entre Lambda e componentes.
wrong.B: Incorreta. CloudTrail audita chamadas de API AWS; não é ferramenta de tracing de performance da aplicação.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Inspector avalia segurança/vulnerabilidades, não traces de performance da Lambda.

question.en: A Developer wants to debug an application by searching and filtering log data. The application logs are stored in Amazon CloudWatch Logs. The Developer creates a new metric filter to count exceptions in the application logs. However, no results are returned from the logs. What is the reason that no filtered results are being returned?
question.pt: Um Developer quer depurar uma aplicação pesquisando e filtrando dados de log. Os logs da aplicação estão armazenados no Amazon CloudWatch Logs. O Developer cria um novo metric filter para contar exceções nos logs da aplicação. Porém, nenhum resultado é retornado dos logs. Qual é o motivo de nenhum resultado filtrado ser retornado?

en: A setup of the Amazon CloudWatch interface VPC endpoint is required for filtering the CloudWatch Logs in the VPC.
CloudWatch Logs only publishes metric data for events that happen after the filter is created.
The log group for CloudWatch Logs should be first streamed to Amazon Elasticsearch Service before metric filtering returns the results.
Metric data points for logs groups can be filtered only after they are exported to an Amazon S3 bucket.
pt: É necessário configurar um VPC endpoint de interface do Amazon CloudWatch para filtrar CloudWatch Logs na VPC.
CloudWatch Logs só publica dados de métricas para eventos que acontecem depois que o filtro é criado.
O log group do CloudWatch Logs deve primeiro ser transmitido para Amazon Elasticsearch Service antes que metric filtering retorne resultados.
Pontos de dados de métricas para log groups só podem ser filtrados depois de exportados para um bucket S3.

correct.B: Correct. Metric filters are not retroactive; they emit metrics only for log events ingested after the filter is created. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/FilterAndPatternSyntax.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. VPC endpoints are unrelated to whether metric filters process historical log events.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Streaming to Elasticsearch/OpenSearch is not required for CloudWatch metric filters.
wrong.D: Incorrect. Exporting logs to S3 is not required before metric filtering.

correct.B: Correta. Metric filters não são retroativos; emitem métricas apenas para eventos ingeridos após a criação do filtro. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/FilterAndPatternSyntax.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. VPC endpoints não têm relação com metric filters processarem eventos históricos.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Transmitir para Elasticsearch/OpenSearch não é necessário para metric filters do CloudWatch.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Exportar logs para S3 não é necessário antes de metric filtering.

question.en: To include objects defined by the AWS Serverless Application Model (SAM) in an AWS CloudFormation template, in addition to `Resources`, what section MUST be included in the document root?
question.pt: Para incluir objetos definidos pelo AWS Serverless Application Model (SAM) em um template AWS CloudFormation, além de `Resources`, qual seção DEVE ser incluída na raiz do documento?

en: `Conditions`.
`Globals`.
`Transform`.
`Properties`.
pt: `Conditions`.
`Globals`.
`Transform`.
`Properties`.

correct.C: Correct. The Transform section, typically AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31, tells CloudFormation to process SAM resources. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/serverless-application-model/latest/developerguide/sam-specification.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Conditions is optional and controls conditional resource creation, not SAM transformation.
wrong.B: Incorrect. Globals is optional in SAM; it is not the required document-root section.
wrong.D: Incorrect. Properties belongs inside resources, not as the required root section for SAM.

correct.C: Correta. A seção Transform, normalmente AWS::Serverless-2016-10-31, instrui CloudFormation a processar recursos SAM. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/serverless-application-model/latest/developerguide/sam-specification.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Conditions é opcional e controla criação condicional, não transformação SAM.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Globals é opcional no SAM; não é a seção obrigatória da raiz.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Properties pertence dentro de recursos, não como seção raiz obrigatória do SAM.

question.en: A company is using Amazon RDS MySQL instances for its application database tier and Apache Tomcat servers for its web tier. Most of the database queries from web applications are repeated read requests. Use of which AWS service would increase in performance by adding in-memory store for repeated read queries?
question.pt: Uma empresa usa instâncias Amazon RDS MySQL para a camada de banco de dados da aplicação e servidores Apache Tomcat para a camada web. A maioria das consultas de banco de dados feitas pelas aplicações web são leituras repetidas. O uso de qual serviço AWS aumentaria a performance ao adicionar um armazenamento em memória para consultas de leitura repetidas?

en: Amazon RDS Multi-AZ.
Amazon SQS.
Amazon ElastiCache.
Amazon RDS read replica.
pt: Amazon RDS Multi-AZ.
Amazon SQS.
Amazon ElastiCache.
Amazon RDS read replica.

correct.C: Correct. ElastiCache provides in-memory caching for repeated reads, reducing load and latency against RDS MySQL. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. RDS Multi-AZ improves availability/failover, not read-query caching.
wrong.B: Incorrect. SQS queues messages asynchronously and does not cache repeated database query results.
wrong.D: Incorrect. A read replica can offload reads, but it is not an in-memory cache and may still query storage.

correct.C: Correta. ElastiCache fornece cache em memória para leituras repetidas, reduzindo carga e latência no RDS MySQL. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. RDS Multi-AZ melhora disponibilidade/failover, não cache de consultas de leitura.
wrong.B: Incorreta. SQS enfileira mensagens de forma assíncrona e não faz cache de consultas de banco.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Read replica descarrega leituras, mas não é cache em memória e ainda consulta armazenamento.

question.en: A Developer is investigating an issue whereby certain requests are passing through an Amazon API Gateway endpoint /MyAPI, but the requests do not reach the AWS Lambda function backing /MyAPI. The Developer found that a second Lambda function sometimes runs at maximum concurrency allowed for the given AWS account. How can the Developer address this issue?
question.pt: Um Developer está investigando um problema em que certas requisições passam por um endpoint Amazon API Gateway `/MyAPI`, mas não chegam à função AWS Lambda que atende `/MyAPI`. O Developer descobriu que uma segunda função Lambda às vezes executa no máximo de concorrência permitido para a conta AWS. Como o Developer pode resolver esse problema?

en: Manually reduce the concurrent execution limit at the account level.
Add another API Gateway stage for /MyAPI, and shard the requests.
Configure the second Lambda function's concurrency execution limit.
Reduce the throttling limits in the API Gateway /MyAPI endpoint
pt: Reduzir manualmente o limite de execução concorrente no nível da conta.
Adicionar outro stage do API Gateway para `/MyAPI` e particionar as requisições.
Configurar o limite de execução concorrente da segunda função Lambda.
Reduzir os limites de throttling no endpoint API Gateway `/MyAPI`.

correct.C: Correct. Setting reserved concurrency on the second Lambda caps its usage, leaving concurrency available for /MyAPI Lambda. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/lambda-concurrency.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Reducing account concurrency would worsen throttling for all functions.
wrong.B: Incorrect. Adding API Gateway stages does not reserve Lambda concurrency or stop another function from consuming it.
wrong.D: Incorrect. Reducing API Gateway throttling limits decreases traffic but does not address account-level Lambda concurrency starvation.

correct.C: Correta. Definir reserved concurrency na segunda Lambda limita seu uso, deixando concorrência disponível para a Lambda de /MyAPI. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/lambda-concurrency.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Reduzir concorrência da conta pioraria throttling para todas as funções.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Adicionar stages no API Gateway não reserva concorrência da Lambda nem impede outra função de consumi-la.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Reduzir throttling no API Gateway diminui tráfego, mas não resolve esgotamento de concorrência da conta Lambda.

question.en: A company is migrating a single-server, on-premises web application to AWS. The company intends to use multiple servers behind an Elastic Load Balancer (ELB) to balance the load, and will also store session data in memory on the web server. The company does not want to lose that session data if a server fails or goes offline, and it wants to minimize user's downtime. Where should the company move session data to MOST effectively reduce downtime and make users' session data more fault tolerant?
question.pt: Uma empresa está migrando para a AWS uma aplicação web on-premises de servidor único. A empresa pretende usar vários servidores atrás de um Elastic Load Balancer (ELB) e também armazenará dados de sessão em memória no web server. A empresa não quer perder esses dados de sessão se um servidor falhar ou ficar offline, e deseja minimizar o downtime dos usuários. Para onde a empresa deve mover os dados de sessão para reduzir downtime e tornar as sessões mais tolerantes a falhas?

en: An Amazon ElastiCache for Redis cluster.
A second Amazon EBS volume.
The web server's primary disk.
An Amazon EC2 instance dedicated to session data.
pt: Um cluster Amazon ElastiCache for Redis.
Um segundo volume Amazon EBS.
O disco primário do web server.
Uma instância Amazon EC2 dedicada para dados de sessão.

correct.A: Correct. ElastiCache for Redis provides shared, in-memory, low-latency session storage with replication/failover options. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/x-forwarded-headers.html

wrong.B: Incorrect. An EBS volume is attached to an instance and does not provide shared fault-tolerant sessions across web servers.
wrong.C: Incorrect. The primary disk fails with the web server and keeps session state local.
wrong.D: Incorrect. A single EC2 session server becomes a bottleneck and single point of failure unless extra architecture is added.

correct.A: Correta. ElastiCache for Redis fornece sessões compartilhadas em memória, baixa latência e opções de replicação/failover. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/x-forwarded-headers.html

wrong.B: Incorreta. Um volume EBS fica anexado a uma instância e não fornece sessões compartilhadas tolerantes a falhas.
wrong.C: Incorreta. O disco primário falha junto com o web server e mantém sessão local.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Uma única EC2 para sessões vira gargalo e ponto único de falha sem arquitetura adicional.

question.en: A Developer created configuration specifications for an AWS Elastic Beanstalk application in a file named healthcheckurl.yaml in the `.ebextensions/directory` of their application source bundle. The file contains the following: After the application launches, the health check is not being run on the correct path, even though it is valid. What can be done to correct this configuration file?
question.pt: Um Developer criou especificações de configuração para uma aplicação AWS Elastic Beanstalk em um arquivo chamado `healthcheckurl.yaml` no diretório `.ebextensions/` do source bundle da aplicação. Depois que a aplicação é iniciada, o health check não está sendo executado no caminho correto, embora o caminho seja válido. O que pode ser feito para corrigir esse arquivo de configuração?

en: Convert the file to JSON format.
Rename the file to a `.config` extension.
Change the configuration section from `options_settings` to resources.
Change the namespace of the option settings to a custom namespace.
pt: Converter o arquivo para o formato JSON.
Renomear o arquivo para uma extensão `.config`.
Alterar a seção de configuração de `options_settings` para `resources`.
Alterar o namespace das option settings para um namespace customizado.

correct.B: Correct. Elastic Beanstalk processes .ebextensions files only when they use the .config extension. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/ebextensions.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Elastic Beanstalk supports YAML or JSON config files, so JSON conversion is not required.
wrong.C: Incorrect. resources is for CloudFormation resources; health check option settings belong in option_settings.
wrong.D: Incorrect. The issue described is file recognition by extension, not a custom namespace requirement.

correct.B: Correta. Elastic Beanstalk processa arquivos em .ebextensions apenas quando usam extensão .config. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/ebextensions.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Elastic Beanstalk suporta YAML ou JSON em config files, então converter para JSON não é necessário.
wrong.C: Incorreta. resources é para recursos CloudFormation; health check fica em option_settings.
wrong.D: Incorreta. O problema é reconhecimento do arquivo pela extensão, não necessidade de namespace customizado.

question.en: A Developer has created a Lambda function and is finding that the function is taking longer to complete than expected. After some debugging, the Developer has discovered that increasing compute capacity would improve performance. How can the Developer increase the Lambda compute resources?
question.pt: Um Developer criou uma função Lambda e percebeu que ela está demorando mais do que o esperado para concluir. Após depuração, descobriu que aumentar a capacidade computacional melhoraria a performance. Como o Developer pode aumentar os recursos computacionais da Lambda?

en: Run on a larger instance size with more compute capacity.
Increase the maximum execution time.
Specify a larger compute capacity when calling the Lambda function.
Increase the allocated memory for the Lambda function.
pt: Executar em uma instância maior com mais capacidade computacional.
Aumentar o tempo máximo de execução.
Especificar maior capacidade computacional ao chamar a função Lambda.
Aumentar a memória alocada para a função Lambda.

correct.D: Correct. Lambda CPU and other compute resources scale with the configured memory allocation. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-function-common.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Lambda does not run on user-selected EC2 instance sizes.
wrong.B: Incorrect. Increasing timeout allows longer execution but does not add CPU.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Compute capacity is configured on the function, not specified per invocation by the caller.

correct.D: Correta. CPU e outros recursos computacionais da Lambda escalam com a memória configurada. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-function-common.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Lambda não roda em tamanhos de instância EC2 escolhidos pelo usuário.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Aumentar timeout permite execução mais longa, mas não adiciona CPU.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Capacidade computacional é configurada na função, não por chamada do invocador.

question.en: An e-commerce site allows returning users to log in to display customized web pages. The workflow is shown in the image below. An application is running on EC2 instances. Amazon RDS is used for the database that stores user accounts and preferences. The website freezes or is slow to load while waiting for the login step to complete. The remaining components of the site are well-optimized. Which of the following techniques will resolve this issue? (Select TWO)
question.pt: Um site de e-commerce permite que usuários recorrentes façam login para exibir páginas personalizadas. Uma aplicação roda em instâncias EC2. Amazon RDS é usado como banco de dados para armazenar contas e preferências dos usuários. O site congela ou carrega lentamente enquanto aguarda a etapa de login. Os demais componentes do site estão bem otimizados. Quais técnicas resolverão esse problema? (Escolha DUAS)

en: Implement the user login page as an asynchronous Lambda function.
Use Amazon ElastiCache for MemCached to cache user data.
Use Amazon Application Load Balancer to load balance the traffic to the website.
Call the database asynchronously so the code can continue executing.
Batch login requests from hundreds of users together as a single read request to the database.
pt: Implementar a página de login do usuário como uma função Lambda assíncrona.
Usar Amazon ElastiCache for Memcached para armazenar dados de usuário em cache.
Usar Amazon Application Load Balancer para balancear o tráfego para o site.
Chamar o banco de dados de forma assíncrona para que o código continue executando.
Agrupar requisições de login de centenas de usuários como uma única leitura no banco de dados.

correct.B: Correct. ElastiCache can cache frequently read user account/preference data, reducing repeated RDS reads during login. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html
correct.D: Correct. Asynchronous database calls let the application continue nondependent work instead of freezing while waiting for I/O. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. An asynchronous Lambda login page would not solve synchronous user authentication latency and may complicate the flow.
wrong.C: Incorrect. An ALB distributes web traffic, but the bottleneck described is waiting on the login/database step.
wrong.E: Incorrect. Batching logins from hundreds of users is not practical for personalized authentication and would add latency.

correct.B: Correta. ElastiCache pode armazenar em cache dados de conta/preferências, reduzindo leituras repetidas no RDS durante login. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html
correct.D: Correta. Chamadas assíncronas ao banco permitem continuar trabalho não dependente em vez de congelar aguardando I/O. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Uma página de login assíncrona em Lambda não resolve latência da autenticação síncrona e pode complicar o fluxo.
wrong.C: Incorreta. ALB distribui tráfego web, mas o gargalo descrito está na etapa de login/banco.
wrong.E: Incorreta. Agrupar logins de centenas de usuários não é prático para autenticação personalizada e adicionaria latência.

question.en: A company maintains a REST service using Amazon API Gateway and the API Gateway native API key validation. The company recently launched a new registration page, which allows users to sign up for the service. The registration page creates a new API key using `CreateApiKey` and sends the new key to the user. When the user attempts to call the API using this key, the user receives a `403 Forbidden` error. Existing users are unaffected and can still call the API. What code updates will grant these new users access to the API?
question.pt: Uma empresa mantém um serviço REST usando Amazon API Gateway e a validação nativa de API key. A página de cadastro cria uma nova API key com `CreateApiKey`, mas novos usuários recebem `403 Forbidden`, enquanto usuários antigos continuam funcionando. Que atualização no código concede acesso aos novos usuários?

en: The `createDeployment` method must be called so the API can be redeployed to include the newly created API key.
The `updateAuthorizer` method must be called to update the API's authorizer to include the newly created API key.
The `importApiKeys` method must be called to import all newly created API keys into the current stage of the API.
The `createUsagePlanKey` method must be called to associate the newly created API key with the correct usage plan.
pt: O `createDeployment` method deve be called so o API can be reimplantared para include o newly criard API key.
O `updateAuthorizer` method deve be called para update o API's authorizer para include o newly criard API key.
O `importApiKeys` method deve be called para import all newly criard API keys into o current stage of o API.
O `createUsagePlanKey` method deve be called para associate o newly criard API key com o correct usage plan.

correct.D: Correct. API Gateway API keys do not grant access by creation alone. After `CreateApiKey`, the key must be associated with a usage plan by `createUsagePlanKey`; otherwise the request can receive `403 Forbidden`. Concept assessed: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Exam trap: confusing key creation with usage-plan association. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/api-gateway-api-usage-plans.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Redeploying publishes API configuration but does not attach a key to a usage plan. Concept assessed: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Exam trap: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.
wrong.B: Incorrect. Authorizers validate identities; API key usage plans are separate. Concept assessed: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Exam trap: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Importing keys is not the same as associating an existing key to a plan. Concept assessed: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Exam trap: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.

correct.D: Correta. API keys do API Gateway não concedem acesso apenas por serem criadas. Depois de `CreateApiKey`, a chave precisa ser associada a um usage plan com `createUsagePlanKey`; caso contrário, a requisição pode receber `403 Forbidden`. Conceito avaliado: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Armadilha da prova: confusing key creation with usage-plan association. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/api-gateway-api-usage-plans.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Reimplantar publica configuração da API, mas não associa a chave a um usage plan. Conceito avaliado: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Armadilha da prova: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Authorizers validam identidades; usage plans de API key são separados. Conceito avaliado: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Armadilha da prova: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Importar chaves não é o mesmo que associar uma chave existente a um plano. Conceito avaliado: API Gateway API keys and usage plans. Armadilha da prova: confusing key creation with usage-plan association.

question.en: A Developer is writing a mobile application that allows users to view images from an S3 bucket. The users must be able to log in with their Amazon login, as well as Facebook and/or Google accounts. How can the Developer provide this authentication functionality?
question.pt: Um Developer is writing a mobile application that permitirs usuários para view images de an S3 bucket. O usuários deve be able para log em com their Amazon login, como well como Facebook and/or Google accounts. Como o Developer pode provide this authentication funçãoality?

en: Use Amazon Cognito with web identity federation.
Use Amazon Cognito with SAML-based identity federation.
Use AWS IAM Access/Secret keys in the application code to allow `Get*` on the S3 bucket.
Use AWS STS `AssumeRole` in the application code and assume a role with `Get*` permissions on the S3 bucket.
pt: Use Amazon Cognito com web identity federation.
Use Amazon Cognito com SAML-based identity federation.
Use AWS IAM Access/Secret keys em o application código para permitir `Get*` em o S3 bucket.
Use AWS STS `AssumeRole` em o application código e assume a role com `Get*` permissions em o S3 bucket.

correct.A: Correct. Amazon Cognito supports web identity federation with social providers such as Login with Amazon, Facebook, and Google, and can issue temporary AWS credentials for S3 access. Concept assessed: Cognito federation. Exam trap: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_providers_oidc.html

wrong.B: Incorrect. SAML targets enterprise federation, not Amazon/Facebook/Google social login. Concept assessed: Cognito federation. Exam trap: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Embedding long-lived AWS keys in app code is insecure. Concept assessed: Cognito federation. Exam trap: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.
wrong.D: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (Cognito federation). The correct mechanism is required because amazon Cognito supports web identity federation with social providers such as Login with Amazon, Facebook, and Google, and can issue temporary AWS credentials for S3 access. Concept assessed: Cognito federation. Exam trap: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.

correct.A: Correta. Amazon Cognito suporta web identity federation com provedores sociais como Login with Amazon, Facebook e Google, e pode emitir credenciais temporárias AWS para acesso ao S3. Conceito avaliado: Cognito federation. Armadilha da prova: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_providers_oidc.html

wrong.B: Incorreta. SAML é para federação corporativa, não login social Amazon/Facebook/Google. Conceito avaliado: Cognito federation. Armadilha da prova: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Embutir chaves AWS de longa duração no código é inseguro. Conceito avaliado: Cognito federation. Armadilha da prova: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (Cognito federation). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque amazon Cognito suporta web identity federation com provedores sociais como Login with Amazon, Facebook e Google, e pode emitir credenciais temporárias AWS para acesso ao S3. Conceito avaliado: Cognito federation. Armadilha da prova: using SAML or embedded keys for social mobile login.

question.en: A Developer wants access to make the log data of an application running on an EC2 instance available to systems administrators. Which of the following enables monitoring of this metric in Amazon CloudWatch?
question.pt: Um Developer wants acessar para make o log dados of an application executada em an EC2 instância available para systems administrators. Quais das seguintes opções habilitars monitoring of this métrica em Amazon CloudWatch?

en: Retrieve the log data from CloudWatch using the `GetMetricData` API call.
Retrieve the log data from AWS CloudTrail using the `LookupEvents` API call.
Launch a new EC2 instance, configure Amazon CloudWatch Events, and then install the application.
Install the Amazon CloudWatch Logs agent on the EC2 instance that the application is running on.
pt: Retrieve o log dados de CloudWatch usando o `GetMetricData` API call.
Retrieve o log dados de AWS CloudTrail usando o `LookupEvents` API call.
Launch a new EC2 instância, configurar Amazon CloudWatch Events, e then install o application.
Install o Amazon CloudWatch Logs agent em o EC2 instância that o application is executada em.

correct.D: Correct. EC2 application logs are not collected automatically. The CloudWatch Logs agent ships application log files to CloudWatch Logs for search, monitoring, metrics, and alarms. Concept assessed: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Exam trap: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/AlarmThatSendsEmail.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (CloudWatch Logs on EC2). The correct mechanism is required because eC2 application logs are not collected automatically. The CloudWatch Logs agent ships application log files to CloudWatch Logs for search, monitoring, metrics, and alarms. Concept assessed: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Exam trap: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.
wrong.B: Incorrect. CloudTrail audits AWS API calls, not application log files. Concept assessed: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Exam trap: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.
wrong.C: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (CloudWatch Logs on EC2). The correct mechanism is required because eC2 application logs are not collected automatically. The CloudWatch Logs agent ships application log files to CloudWatch Logs for search, monitoring, metrics, and alarms. Concept assessed: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Exam trap: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.

correct.D: Correta. Logs de aplicação em EC2 não são coletados automaticamente. O CloudWatch Logs agent envia os logs para CloudWatch Logs para busca, monitoramento, métricas e alarmes. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Armadilha da prova: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/AlarmThatSendsEmail.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (CloudWatch Logs on EC2). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque logs de aplicação em EC2 não são coletados automaticamente. O CloudWatch Logs agent envia os logs para CloudWatch Logs para busca, monitoramento, métricas e alarmes. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Armadilha da prova: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.
wrong.B: Incorreta. CloudTrail audita APIs AWS, não arquivos de log de aplicação. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Armadilha da prova: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (CloudWatch Logs on EC2). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque logs de aplicação em EC2 não são coletados automaticamente. O CloudWatch Logs agent envia os logs para CloudWatch Logs para busca, monitoramento, métricas e alarmes. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch Logs on EC2. Armadilha da prova: confusing metrics/API audit events with log ingestion.

question.en: A nightly batch job loads 1 million new records into a DynamoDB table. The records are only needed for one hour, and the table needs to be empty by the next night's batch job. Which is the MOST efficient and cost-effective method to provide an empty table?
question.pt: Um job em lote noturno carrega 1 milhão de novos registros em uma tabela do DynamoDB. Os registros são necessários por apenas uma hora, e a tabela precisa estar vazia antes do próximo job noturno. Qual é o método MAIS eficiente e econômico para disponibilizar a tabela vazia?

en: Use `DeleteItem` using a `ConditionExpression`.
Use `BatchWriteItem` to empty all of the rows.
Write a recursive function that scans and calls out `DeleteItem`.
Create and then delete the table after the task has completed.
pt: Usar `DeleteItem` com uma `ConditionExpression`.
Usar `BatchWriteItem` para esvaziar todas as linhas.
Escrever uma função recursiva que faz scan e chama `DeleteItem`.
Criar e depois excluir a tabela após a conclusão da tarefa.

correct.D: Correct. Deleting and recreating the DynamoDB table avoids scanning and deleting one million items, which would consume write capacity and many API calls. Concept assessed: DynamoDB bulk cleanup. Exam trap: deleting every item individually. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/Scan.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Item-by-item deletion consumes capacity and API calls. Concept assessed: DynamoDB bulk cleanup. Exam trap: deleting every item individually.
wrong.B: Incorrect. BatchWriteItem still deletes item by item and consumes write capacity. Concept assessed: DynamoDB bulk cleanup. Exam trap: deleting every item individually.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Item-by-item deletion consumes capacity and API calls. Concept assessed: DynamoDB bulk cleanup. Exam trap: deleting every item individually.

correct.D: Correta. Excluir e recriar a tabela DynamoDB evita varrer e excluir um milhão de itens, o que consumiria write capacity e muitas chamadas de API. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB bulk cleanup. Armadilha da prova: deleting every item individually. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/ProvisionedThroughput.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Delete por item consome capacidade e chamadas de API. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB bulk cleanup. Armadilha da prova: deleting every item individually.
wrong.B: Incorreta. BatchWriteItem ainda exclui item por item e consome WCU. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB bulk cleanup. Armadilha da prova: deleting every item individually.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Delete por item consome capacidade e chamadas de API. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB bulk cleanup. Armadilha da prova: deleting every item individually.

question.en: A company has an application that logs all information to Amazon S3. Whenever there is a new log file, an AWS Lambda function is invoked to process the log files. The code works, gathering all of the necessary information. However, when checking the Lambda function logs, duplicate entries with the same request ID are found. What is causing the duplicate entries?
question.pt: Uma empresa tem uma aplicação que registra todas as informações no Amazon S3. Sempre que há um novo arquivo de log, uma função AWS Lambda é invocada para processar os arquivos de log. O código funciona, coletando todas as informações necessárias. No entanto, ao verificar os logs da função Lambda, são encontradas entradas duplicadas com o mesmo request ID. O que está causando as entradas duplicadas?

en: The S3 bucket name was specified incorrectly.
The Lambda function failed, and the Lambda service retired the invocation with a delay.
There was an S3 outage, which caused duplicate entries of the sale log file.
The application stopped intermittently and then resumed.
pt: O nome do bucket S3 foi especificado incorretamente.
A função Lambda falhou e o serviço Lambda repetiu a invocação com atraso.
Houve uma falha no S3 que causou entradas duplicadas do mesmo arquivo de log.
A aplicação parou intermitentemente e depois foi retomada.

correct.B: Correct. S3-to-Lambda asynchronous invocations are at-least-once. If the function fails or times out, Lambda can retry the same event and create duplicate processing. Concept assessed: Lambda retry behavior. Exam trap: assuming exactly-once event processing. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/invocation-retries.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (Lambda retry behavior). The correct mechanism is required because s3-to-Lambda asynchronous invocations are at-least-once. If the function fails or times out, Lambda can retry the same event and create duplicate processing. Concept assessed: Lambda retry behavior. Exam trap: assuming exactly-once event processing.
wrong.C: Incorrect. The symptom matches Lambda retry, not an S3 outage. Concept assessed: Lambda retry behavior. Exam trap: assuming exactly-once event processing.
wrong.D: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (Lambda retry behavior). The correct mechanism is required because s3-to-Lambda asynchronous invocations are at-least-once. If the function fails or times out, Lambda can retry the same event and create duplicate processing. Concept assessed: Lambda retry behavior. Exam trap: assuming exactly-once event processing.

correct.B: Correta. Invocações assíncronas S3 para Lambda têm semântica at-least-once. Se a função falha ou expira, a Lambda pode repetir o mesmo evento e gerar duplicidade. Conceito avaliado: Lambda retry behavior. Armadilha da prova: assuming exactly-once event processing. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/invocation-retries.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (Lambda retry behavior). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque invocações assíncronas S3 para Lambda têm semântica at-least-once. Se a função falha ou expira, a Lambda pode repetir o mesmo evento e gerar duplicidade. Conceito avaliado: Lambda retry behavior. Armadilha da prova: assuming exactly-once event processing.
wrong.C: Incorreta. O sintoma combina com retry da Lambda, não outage do S3. Conceito avaliado: Lambda retry behavior. Armadilha da prova: assuming exactly-once event processing.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (Lambda retry behavior). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque invocações assíncronas S3 para Lambda têm semântica at-least-once. Se a função falha ou expira, a Lambda pode repetir o mesmo evento e gerar duplicidade. Conceito avaliado: Lambda retry behavior. Armadilha da prova: assuming exactly-once event processing.

question.en: A company is providing services to many downstream consumers. Each consumer may connect to one or more services. This has resulted in a complex architecture that is difficult to manage and does not scale well. The company needs a single interface to manage these services to consumers. Which AWS service should be used to refactor this architecture?
question.pt: Uma empresa fornece serviços para muitos consumidores downstream. Cada consumidor pode se conectar a um ou mais serviços. Isso resultou em uma arquitetura complexa, difícil de gerenciar e que não escala bem. A empresa precisa de uma interface única para gerenciar esses serviços para os consumidores. Qual serviço AWS deve ser usado para refatorar essa arquitetura?

en: AWS Lambda.
AWS X-Ray.
Amazon SQS.
Amazon API Gateway.
pt: AWS Lambda.
AWS X-Ray.
Amazon SQS.
Amazon API Gateway.

correct.D: Correct. API Gateway is a managed API front door that centralizes routing, throttling, authorization, stages, and integrations for many backend services. Concept assessed: API facade. Exam trap: choosing compute/tracing/queues for API management. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/welcome.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Lambda runs code but is not an API management facade. Concept assessed: API facade. Exam trap: choosing compute/tracing/queues for API management.
wrong.B: Incorrect. X-Ray traces requests; it does not front APIs. Concept assessed: API facade. Exam trap: choosing compute/tracing/queues for API management.
wrong.C: Incorrect. SQS queues messages; it does not manage REST APIs. Concept assessed: API facade. Exam trap: choosing compute/tracing/queues for API management.

correct.D: Correta. API Gateway é uma porta de entrada gerenciada para APIs que centraliza roteamento, throttling, autorização, stages e integrações para muitos backends. Conceito avaliado: API facade. Armadilha da prova: choosing compute/tracing/queues for API management. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/welcome.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Lambda executa código, mas não é fachada de gerenciamento de API. Conceito avaliado: API facade. Armadilha da prova: choosing compute/tracing/queues for API management.
wrong.B: Incorreta. X-Ray rastreia requisições; não expõe APIs. Conceito avaliado: API facade. Armadilha da prova: choosing compute/tracing/queues for API management.
wrong.C: Incorreta. SQS enfileira mensagens; não gerencia APIs REST. Conceito avaliado: API facade. Armadilha da prova: choosing compute/tracing/queues for API management.

question.en: A Developer is creating a serverless website with content that includes HTML files, images, videos, and JavaScript (client-side scripts). Which combination of services should the Developer use to create the website?
question.pt: Um Developer is creating a serverless website com content that includes HTML arquivos, images, videos, e JavaScript (client-side scripts). Qual combinação de serviços should o Developer use para criar o website?

en: Amazon S3 and Amazon CloudFront.
Amazon EC2 and Amazon ElastiCache.
Amazon ECS and Redis.
AWS Lambda and Amazon API Gateway.
pt: Amazon S3 e Amazon CloudFront.
Amazon EC2 e Amazon ElastiCache.
Amazon ECS e Redis.
AWS Lambda e Amazon API Gateway.

correct.A: Correct. S3 serves static assets and CloudFront distributes/cache them globally, which fits HTML, images, videos, and client-side JavaScript without servers. Concept assessed: static website hosting. Exam trap: adding compute for static content. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/WebsiteHosting.html

wrong.B: Incorrect. EC2 adds server management and is unnecessary for this managed/serverless requirement. Concept assessed: static website hosting. Exam trap: adding compute for static content.
wrong.C: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (static website hosting). The correct mechanism is required because s3 serves static assets and CloudFront distributes/cache them globally, which fits HTML, images, videos, and client-side JavaScript without servers. Concept assessed: static website hosting. Exam trap: adding compute for static content.
wrong.D: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (static website hosting). The correct mechanism is required because s3 serves static assets and CloudFront distributes/cache them globally, which fits HTML, images, videos, and client-side JavaScript without servers. Concept assessed: static website hosting. Exam trap: adding compute for static content.

correct.A: Correta. S3 serve assets estáticos e CloudFront distribui/cacheia globalmente, atendendo HTML, imagens, vídeos e JavaScript client-side sem servidores. Conceito avaliado: static website hosting. Armadilha da prova: adding compute for static content. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/WebsiteHosting.html

wrong.B: Incorreta. EC2 adiciona servidores e é desnecessário para esse requisito gerenciado/serverless. Conceito avaliado: static website hosting. Armadilha da prova: adding compute for static content.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (static website hosting). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque s3 serve assets estáticos e CloudFront distribui/cacheia globalmente, atendendo HTML, imagens, vídeos e JavaScript client-side sem servidores. Conceito avaliado: static website hosting. Armadilha da prova: adding compute for static content.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (static website hosting). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque s3 serve assets estáticos e CloudFront distribui/cacheia globalmente, atendendo HTML, imagens, vídeos e JavaScript client-side sem servidores. Conceito avaliado: static website hosting. Armadilha da prova: adding compute for static content.

question.en: A Development team has pushed out 10 applications running on several Amazon EC2 instances. The Operations team is asking for a graphical representation of one key performance metric for each application. These metrics should be available on one screen for easy monitoring. Which steps should the Developer take to accomplish this using Amazon CloudWatch?
question.pt: A equipe de desenvolvimento has pushed out 10 applications executada em several Amazon EC2 instâncias. O Operations team is asking para a graphical representation of one key performance métrica para each application. These métricas should be available em one screen para easy monitoring. Qual etapas should o Developer take para accomplish thusa Amazon CloudWatch?

en: Create a custom namespace with a unique metric name for each application.
Create a custom dimension with a unique metric name for each application.
Create a custom event with a unique metric name for each application.
Create a custom alarm with a unique metric name for each application.
pt: Create a customizado namespace com a unique métrica name para each application.
Create a customizado dimension com a unique métrica name para each application.
Create a customizado event com a unique métrica name para each application.
Create a customizado alarm com a unique métrica name para each application.

correct.A: Correct. Custom CloudWatch metrics in a namespace with unique metric names let operations graph each application metric on a single dashboard. Concept assessed: CloudWatch custom metrics. Exam trap: confusing alarms/events with metric definition. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/monitoring/publishingMetrics.html

wrong.B: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (CloudWatch custom metrics). The correct mechanism is required because custom CloudWatch metrics in a namespace with unique metric names let operations graph each application metric on a single dashboard. Concept assessed: CloudWatch custom metrics. Exam trap: confusing alarms/events with metric definition.
wrong.C: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (CloudWatch custom metrics). The correct mechanism is required because custom CloudWatch metrics in a namespace with unique metric names let operations graph each application metric on a single dashboard. Concept assessed: CloudWatch custom metrics. Exam trap: confusing alarms/events with metric definition.
wrong.D: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (CloudWatch custom metrics). The correct mechanism is required because custom CloudWatch metrics in a namespace with unique metric names let operations graph each application metric on a single dashboard. Concept assessed: CloudWatch custom metrics. Exam trap: confusing alarms/events with metric definition.

correct.A: Correta. Métricas customizadas do CloudWatch em um namespace com nomes únicos permitem exibir a métrica de cada aplicação em um único dashboard. question.en: A company uses Amazon DynamoDB for managing and tracking orders. The DynamoDB table is partitioned based on the order date. The company receives a huge increase in orders during a sales event, causing DynamoDB writes to throttle, and the consumed throughput is far below the provisioned throughput. According to AWS best practices, how can this issue be resolved with MINIMAL costs?

A empresa recebe um aumento enorme de pedidos durante um evento de vendas, fazendo com que as gravações no DynamoDB sofram throttling, e o throughput consumido está muito abaixo do throughput provisionado. De acordo com as melhores práticas da AWS, como esse problema pode ser resolvido com o MENOR custo?

en: Create a new DynamoDB table for every order date.
Increase the read and write capacity units of the DynamoDB table.
Add a random number suffix to the partition key values.
Add a global secondary index to the DynamoDB table.

Aumentar as unidades de capacidade de leitura e gravação da tabela DynamoDB.
Adicionar um sufixo de número aleatório aos valores da partition key.
Adicionar um índice secundário global à tabela DynamoDB.

correct.C: Correct. Partitioning by date creates a hot partition during sales spikes. Adding a random suffix spreads writes across partition key values with minimal cost. Concept assessed: DynamoDB hot partitions. Exam trap: buying capacity when key design is the issue. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/ProvisionedThroughput.html

question.en: A Development team currently supports an application that uses an in-memory store to save accumulated game results. Individual results are stored in a database. As part of migrating to AWS, the team needs to use automatic scaling. The team knows this will yield inconsistent results. Where should the team store these accumulated game results to BEST allow for consistent results without impacting performance?
question.pt: Uma equipe de desenvolvimento mantém uma aplicação que usa um armazenamento em memória para salvar resultados acumulados de jogos. Os resultados individuais são armazenados em um banco de dados. Como parte da migração para a AWS, a equipe precisa usar escalonamento automático. A equipe sabe que isso causará resultados inconsistentes. Onde a equipe deve armazenar esses resultados acumulados de jogos para MELHOR permitir resultados consistentes sem impactar o desempenho?

en: Amazon S3.
Amazon RDS.
Amazon ElastiCache.
Amazon Kinesis.
pt: Amazon S3.
Amazon RDS.
Amazon ElastiCache.
Amazon Kinesis.

correct.C: Correct. ElastiCache is a shared low-latency in-memory store, avoiding inconsistent per-instance memory while preserving performance under Auto Scaling. Concept assessed: shared in-memory state. Exam trap: keeping accumulated state in local memory. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (shared in-memory state). The correct mechanism is required because elastiCache is a shared low-latency in-memory store, avoiding inconsistent per-instance memory while preserving performance under Auto Scaling. Concept assessed: shared in-memory state. Exam trap: keeping accumulated state in local memory.
wrong.B: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: shared in-memory state. Exam trap: keeping accumulated state in local memory.
wrong.D: Incorrect. Kinesis streams records; it is not a shared state/cache store. Concept assessed: shared in-memory state. Exam trap: keeping accumulated state in local memory.

correct.C: Correta. ElastiCache é um armazenamento em memória compartilhado e de baixa latência, evitando memória local inconsistente e mantendo performance com Auto Scaling. Conceito avaliado: shared in-memory state. Armadilha da prova: keeping accumulated state in local memory. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (shared in-memory state). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque elastiCache é um armazenamento em memória compartilhado e de baixa latência, evitando memória local inconsistente e mantendo performance com Auto Scaling. Conceito avaliado: shared in-memory state. Armadilha da prova: keeping accumulated state in local memory.
wrong.B: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: shared in-memory state. Armadilha da prova: keeping accumulated state in local memory.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Kinesis transmite registros; não é cache/estado compartilhado. Conceito avaliado: shared in-memory state. Armadilha da prova: keeping accumulated state in local memory.

question.en: An application that runs on an Amazon EC2 instance needs to access and make API calls to multiple AWS services. What is the MOST secure way to provide access to the AWS services with MINIMAL management overhead?
question.pt: Uma aplicação that executa em an Amazon EC2 instância precisa acessar e make API calls para multiple AWS services. Qual é a forma MAIS seguro way para provide acessar para o AWS services com MINIMAL management overhead?

en: Use AWS KMS to store and retrieve credentials.
Use EC2 instance profiles.
Use AWS `root` user to make requests to the application.
Store and retrieve credentials from AWS CodeCommit.
pt: Use AWS KMS para armazenar e recuperar credenciais.
Use EC2 instância proarquivos.
Use AWS `root` user para make requests para o application.
Store e recuperar credenciais de AWS CodeCommit.

correct.B: Correct. EC2 instance profiles provide temporary IAM role credentials through instance metadata, avoiding static keys and reducing management overhead. Concept assessed: IAM roles for EC2. Exam trap: storing long-lived credentials. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/iam-roles-for-amazon-ec2.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. KMS manages keys, not users/password reset/application directories. Concept assessed: IAM roles for EC2. Exam trap: storing long-lived credentials.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Using the root user violates least privilege. Concept assessed: IAM roles for EC2. Exam trap: storing long-lived credentials.
wrong.D: Incorrect. CodeCommit is source control, not deployment orchestration. Concept assessed: IAM roles for EC2. Exam trap: storing long-lived credentials.

correct.B: Correta. EC2 instance profiles fornecem credenciais temporárias de IAM role via metadata, evitando chaves estáticas e reduzindo gestão. Conceito avaliado: IAM roles for EC2. wrong.A: Incorreta. KMS gerencia chaves, não usuários/reset de senha. Conceito avaliado: IAM roles for EC2. Armadilha da prova: storing long-lived credentials.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Usar root viola least privilege. Conceito avaliado: IAM roles for EC2. Armadilha da prova: storing long-lived credentials.
wrong.D: Incorreta. CodeCommit é controle de fonte, não orquestra implantação. Conceito avaliado: IAM roles for EC2. Armadilha da prova: storing long-lived credentials.

question.en: A company maintains an application responsible for processing several thousand external callbacks each day. The company's System administrators want to know how many callbacks are being received on a rolling basis, and they want this data available for 10 days. The company also wants the ability to issue automated alerts if the number of callbacks exceeds the defined thresholds. What is the MOST cost-effective way to address the need to track and alert on these statistics?
question.pt: Uma empresa mantém uma aplicação responsável por processar vários milhares de callbacks externos por dia. Os administradores de sistema da empresa querem saber quantos callbacks estão sendo recebidos em uma base contínua, e querem esses dados disponíveis por 10 dias. A empresa também quer a capacidade de emitir alertas automáticos se o número de callbacks exceder os limites definidos. Qual é a maneira MAIS econômica de rastrear e alertar sobre essas estatísticas?

en: Push callback data to an Amazon RDS database that can be queried to show historical data and to alert on exceeded thresholds.
Push callback data to AWS X-Ray and use AWS Lambda to query, display, and alert on exceeded thresholds.
Push callback data to Amazon Kinesis Data Streams and invoke an AWS Lambda function that stores data in Amazon DynamoDB and sends the required alerts.
Push callback data to Amazon CloudWatch as a custom metric and use the CloudWatch alerting mechanisms to alert System Administrators.
pt: Enviar dados de callback para um banco de dados Amazon RDS que pode ser consultado para mostrar dados históricos e alertar sobre limites excedidos.
Enviar dados de callback para o AWS X-Ray e usar o AWS Lambda para consultar, exibir e alertar sobre limites excedidos.
Enviar dados de callback para o Amazon Kinesis Data Streams e invocar uma função AWS Lambda que armazena dados no Amazon DynamoDB e envia os alertas necessários.
Enviar dados de callback para o Amazon CloudWatch como uma métrica personalizada e usar os mecanismos de alerta do CloudWatch para notificar os administradores de sistema.

correct.D: Correct. CloudWatch custom metrics are built for numeric time-series, retention, dashboards, and alarms, making callback count tracking and alerting cost-effective. Concept assessed: CloudWatch custom metrics. Exam trap: building databases/streams for simple counters. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: CloudWatch custom metrics. Exam trap: building databases/streams for simple counters.
wrong.B: Incorrect. X-Ray traces requests; it does not front APIs. Concept assessed: CloudWatch custom metrics. Exam trap: building databases/streams for simple counters.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Kinesis streams records; it is not a shared state/cache store. Concept assessed: CloudWatch custom metrics. Exam trap: building databases/streams for simple counters.

correct.D: Correta. CloudWatch custom metrics são feitas para séries temporais numéricas, retenção, dashboards e alarmes, tornando contagem e alerta de callbacks econômicos. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch custom metrics. Armadilha da prova: building databases/streams for simple counters. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch custom metrics. Armadilha da prova: building databases/streams for simple counters.
wrong.B: Incorreta. X-Ray rastreia requisições; não expõe APIs. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch custom metrics. Armadilha da prova: building databases/streams for simple counters.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Kinesis transmite registros; não é cache/estado compartilhado. Conceito avaliado: CloudWatch custom metrics. Armadilha da prova: building databases/streams for simple counters.

wrong.B: Incorreta. Rolling altera capacidade existente e rollback é menos isolado. Conceito avaliado: Elastic Beanstalk deployment policies. Armadilha da prova: using rolling/all-at-once when failure cannot affect users.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (Elastic Beanstalk deployment policies). question.en: A company has a multi-tiered web application on AWS. During a recent spike in traffic, one of the primary relational databases on Amazon RDS could not serve all the traffic. Some read queries for repeatedly accessed items failed, so users received error messages. What can be done to minimize the impact on database read queries MOST efficiently during future traffic spikes?
question.pt: Uma empresa has a multi-tiered web application em AWS. During a recent spike em traffic, one of o primary relational dadosbases em Amazon RDS could not serve all o traffic. Some ler queries para repeatedly acessared items failed, so usuários received error mensagens. What can be done para minimize o impact em dadosbase ler queries MOST eficientely during future traffic spikes?

en: Use Amazon S3 to cache database query results.
Use Amazon RDS as a custom origin for Amazon CloudFront.
Use local storage and memory on Amazon EC2 instances to cache data.
Use Amazon ElastiCache in front of the primary database to cache data.

Use Amazon RDS como a customizado origin para Amazon CloudFront.
Use local storage e memory em Amazon EC2 instâncias para cache dados.
Use Amazon ElastiCache em front of o primary dadosbase para cache dados.

correct.D: Correct. ElastiCache caches hot read results in memory and reduces repeated read load on RDS during traffic spikes. Concept assessed: database caching. question.en: A Developer must build an application that uses Amazon DynamoDB. The requirements state that the items being stored in the DynamoDB table will be 7KB in size and that reads must be strongly consistent. The maximum read rate is 3 items per second, and the maximum write rate is 10 items per second. How should the Developer size the DynamoDB table to meet these requirements?

en: Read: 3 read capacity. `unitsWrite`: 70 write capacity units.
Read: 6 read capacity. `unitsWrite`: 70 write capacity units.
Read: 6 read capacity. `unitsWrite`: 10 write capacity units.
Read: 3 read capacity. `unitsWrite`: 10 write capacity units.

Read: 6 ler capacity. `unitsWrite`: 10 gravar capacity units.
Read: 3 ler capacity. `unitsWrite`: 10 gravar capacity units.

correct.B: Correct. Strong reads use 1 RCU per 4 KB block. 7 KB rounds to 2 RCUs; 3 reads/s need 6 RCUs. Writes use 1 WCU per 1 KB, so 7 KB at 10 writes/s needs 70 WCUs.
