question.en: A Developer is creating an AWS Lambda function to process a stream of data from an Amazon Kinesis Data Stream. When the Lambda function parses the data and encounters a missing field, it exits the function with an error. The function is generating duplicate records from the Kinesis stream. When the Developer looks at the stream output without the Lambda function, there are no duplicate records. What is the reason for the duplicates?
question.pt: Um Developer está criando uma função AWS Lambda para processar um fluxo de dados de um Amazon Kinesis Data Stream. Quando a função Lambda analisa os dados e encontra um campo ausente, ela encerra a função com um erro. A função está gerando registros duplicados do stream do Kinesis. Quando o Developer observa a saída do stream sem a função Lambda, não há registros duplicados. Qual é o motivo das duplicatas?

en: The Lambda function did not advance the Kinesis stream pointer to the next record after the error.
The Lambda event source used asynchronous invocation, resulting in duplicate records.
The Lambda function did not handle the error, and the Lambda service attempted to reprocess the data.
The Lambda function is not keeping up with the amount of data coming from the stream.
pt: A função Lambda não avançou o ponteiro do stream do Kinesis para o próximo registro após o erro.
O event source do Lambda usou invocação assíncrona, resultando em registros duplicados.
A função Lambda não tratou o erro e o serviço Lambda tentou reprocessar os dados.
A função Lambda não está acompanhando a quantidade de dados vinda do stream.

correct.C: Correct. For Kinesis event sources, Lambda retries failed batches. If parsing errors are not handled, the same records can be reprocessed and appear duplicated. Concept assessed: Lambda stream retries. Exam trap: blaming Kinesis for retry duplicates. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Kinesis streams records; it is not a shared state/cache store. Concept assessed: Lambda stream retries. Exam trap: blaming Kinesis for retry duplicates.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Kinesis transmite registros; não é cache/estado compartilhado. Conceito avaliado: Lambda stream retries. Armadilha da prova: blaming Kinesis for retry duplicates.
wrong.B: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: Lambda stream retries. Armadilha da prova: blaming Kinesis for retry duplicates.

question.en: A company is developing an application that will run on several Amazon EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling group and can access a database running on Amazon EC2. The application needs to store secrets required to connect to the database. The application must allow for periodic secret rotation, and there should be no changes to the application when a secret changes. What is the SAFEST way to meet these requirements?

A aplicação precisa armazenar segredos necessários para se conectar ao banco de dados. A aplicação deve permitir a rotação periódica de segredos, e não deve haver alterações na aplicação quando um segredo mudar. Qual é a maneira MAIS SEGURA de atender a esses requisitos?

en: Associate an IAM role to the EC2 instance where the application is running with permission to access the database.
Use AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store with the SecureString data type to store secrets.
Configure the application to store secrets in Amazon S3 object metadata.
Hard code the database secrets in the application code itself.
pt: Associar uma função IAM à instância EC2 onde a aplicação está sendo executada, com permissão para acessar o banco de dados.

Configurar a aplicação para armazenar segredos nos metadados de objetos do Amazon S3.
Codificar os segredos do banco de dados diretamente no próprio código da aplicação.

Call the `create-alias` API.

en: Modify the default configuration for the Lambda function to associate it with an Amazon VPC private subnet.

Add an environmental variable to the Lambda function to allow outbound internet access.

question.en: A Developer has been asked to make changes to the source code of an AWS Lambda function. The function is managed using an AWS CloudFormation template. The template is configured to load the source code from an Amazon S3 bucket. The Developer manually created a `.ZIP` file deployment package containing the changes and put the file into the correct location on Amazon S3. When the function is invoked, the code changes have not been applied. What step is required to update the function with the changes?
question.pt: Um Developer foi solicitado a fazer alterações no código-fonte de uma função AWS Lambda. A função é gerenciada usando um template AWS CloudFormation. O template está configurado para carregar o código-fonte de um bucket Amazon S3. O Developer criou manualmente um pacote de implantação `.ZIP` com as alterações e colocou o arquivo no local correto no Amazon S3. Quando a função é invocada, as alterações de código não foram aplicadas. Qual etapa é necessária para atualizar a função com as alterações?

en: Delete the `.ZIP` file on S3, and re-upload by using a different object key name.

Modify the execution role of the Lambda function to allow S3 access permission to the deployment package `.ZIP` file.

Modificar a execution role da função Lambda para permitir permissão de acesso ao S3 para o arquivo `.ZIP` do pacote de implantação.

question.en: A Developer wants to find a list of items in a global secondary index from an Amazon DynamoDB table. Which DynamoDB API call can the Developer use in order to consume the LEAST number of read capacity units?

en: Scan operation using `eventually-consistent` reads.
Query operation using `strongly-consistent` reads.
Query operation using `eventually-consistent` reads.
Scan operation using `strongly-consistent` reads.

Operação Query usando leituras `strongly-consistent`.
Operação Query usando leituras `eventually-consistent`.
Operação Scan usando leituras `strongly-consistent`.

correct.C: Correct. Query reads only matching indexed items. GSIs support eventually consistent reads only, and Query consumes less capacity than Scan. Concept assessed: DynamoDB Query on GSI. wrong.A: Incorrect. Scan reads broadly and costs more than Query. Concept assessed: DynamoDB Query on GSI. Exam trap: strong reads on GSI or scans.
wrong.B: Incorrect. GSIs do not support strongly consistent reads. Concept assessed: DynamoDB Query on GSI. Exam trap: strong reads on GSI or scans.

wrong.D: Incorreta. Scan lê amplamente e custa mais que Query. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB Query on GSI. Armadilha da prova: strong reads on GSI or scans.

question.en: A Developer has published an update to an application that is served to a global user base using Amazon CloudFront. After deploying the application, users are not able to see the updated changes. How can the Developer resolve this issue?

en: Remove the origin from the CloudFront configuration and add it again.
Disable forwarding of query strings and request headers from the CloudFront distribution configuration.
Invalidate all the application objects from the edge caches.
Disable the CloudFront distribution and enable it again to update all the edge locations.
pt: Remover a origem da configuração do CloudFront e adicioná-la novamente.
Desabilitar o encaminhamento de query strings e cabeçalhos de requisição na configuração da distribuição do CloudFront.
Invalidar todos os objetos da aplicação nos edge caches.
Desabilitar a distribuição do CloudFront e habilitá-la novamente para atualizar todos os edge locations.

correct.C: Correct. CloudFront edge caches can keep old objects. Invalidating objects removes stale cached copies so users get the new version. Concept assessed: CloudFront invalidation. Exam trap: changing distribution instead of invalidating cache. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.D: Incorrect. Lambda runs code but is not an API management facade. Concept assessed: Lambda packages. Exam trap: installing dependencies at invocation time.

correct.A: Correta. O pacote de implantação deve incluir código e dependências para estarem disponíveis quando a Lambda inicia. Conceito avaliado: Lambda packages. Armadilha da prova: installing dependencies at invocation time. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-function-zip.html

wrong.B: Incorreta. O runtime gerenciado não é modificado para bibliotecas da app. Conceito avaliado: Lambda packages. Armadilha da prova: installing dependencies at invocation time.

wrong.D: Incorreta. Lambda executa código, mas não é fachada de gerenciamento de API. Conceito avaliado: Lambda packages. Armadilha da prova: installing dependencies at invocation time.

question.en: A Developer is designing a fault-tolerant environment where client sessions will be saved. How can the Developer ensure that no sessions are lost if an Amazon EC2 instance fails?
question.pt: Um Developer está projetando um ambiente tolerante a falhas onde as sessões de clientes serão salvas. Como o Developer pode garantir que nenhuma sessão seja perdida se uma instância Amazon EC2 falhar?

en: Use sticky sessions with an Elastic Load Balancer target group.
Use Amazon SQS to save session data.
Use Amazon DynamoDB to perform scalable session handling.
Use Elastic Load Balancer connection draining to stop sending requests to failing instances.
pt: Usar sticky sessions com um grupo de destino do Elastic Load Balancer.
Usar Amazon SQS para salvar dados de sessão.
Usar Amazon DynamoDB para realizar o gerenciamento de sessões escalável.
Usar o connection draining do Elastic Load Balancer para parar de enviar requisições para instâncias com falha.

correct.C: Correct. DynamoDB is a durable shared session store; any EC2 instance can retrieve the session after another instance fails. Concept assessed: external session state. Exam trap: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/sticky-sessions.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Sticky sessions still lose data if the instance fails. Concept assessed: external session state. Exam trap: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure.
wrong.B: Incorrect. SQS queues messages; it does not manage REST APIs. Concept assessed: external session state. Exam trap: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure.
wrong.D: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (external session state). The correct mechanism is required because dynamoDB is a durable shared session store; any EC2 instance can retrieve the session after another instance fails. Concept assessed: external session state. Exam trap: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure.

correct.C: Correta. DynamoDB é armazenamento de sessão compartilhado e durável; qualquer EC2 recupera a sessão se outra falhar. Conceito avaliado: external session state. Armadilha da prova: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticloadbalancing/latest/application/sticky-sessions.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Sticky sessions ainda perdem dados se a instância falhar. Conceito avaliado: external session state. Armadilha da prova: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure.
wrong.B: Incorreta. SQS enfileira mensagens; não gerencia APIs REST. Conceito avaliado: external session state. Armadilha da prova: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (external session state). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque dynamoDB é armazenamento de sessão compartilhado e durável; qualquer EC2 recupera a sessão se outra falhar. Conceito avaliado: external session state. Armadilha da prova: sticky/local sessions lose state on failure.

question.en: In a move toward using microservices, a company's Management team has asked all Development teams to build their services so that API requests depend only on that service's data store. One team is building a Payments service which has its own database; the service needs data that originates in the Accounts database. Both are using Amazon DynamoDB. What approach will result in the simplest, decoupled, and reliable method to get near-real time updates from the Accounts database?
question.pt: Em uma transição para microservices, a equipe de gerenciamento de uma empresa pediu a todas as equipes de desenvolvimento que construíssem seus serviços de forma que as requisições de API dependam apenas do armazenamento de dados daquele serviço. Uma equipe está construindo um serviço de Pagamentos com seu próprio banco de dados; o serviço precisa de dados que se originam no banco de dados de Contas. Ambos usam Amazon DynamoDB. Qual abordagem resultará no método mais simples, desacoplado e confiável para obter atualizações em tempo quase real do banco de dados de Contas?

en: Use Amazon Glue to perform frequent ETL updates from the Accounts database to the Payments database.
Use Amazon ElastiCache in Payments, with the cache updated by triggers in the Accounts database.
Use Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose to deliver all changes from the Accounts database to the Payments database.
Use Amazon DynamoDB Streams to deliver all changes from the Accounts database to the Payments database.
pt: Usar o Amazon Glue para realizar atualizações ETL frequentes do banco de dados de Contas para o banco de dados de Pagamentos.
Usar o Amazon ElastiCache em Pagamentos, com o cache atualizado por triggers no banco de dados de Contas.
Usar o Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose para entregar todas as alterações do banco de dados de Contas para o banco de dados de Pagamentos.
Usar o Amazon DynamoDB Streams para entregar todas as alterações do banco de dados de Contas para o banco de dados de Pagamentos.

correct.D: Correct. DynamoDB Streams captures item-level changes and can feed downstream updates to another service database in near real time. Concept assessed: DynamoDB Streams. Exam trap: batch ETL or cache for change propagation. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Glue is batch ETL, not near-real-time DynamoDB change propagation. Concept assessed: DynamoDB Streams. Exam trap: batch ETL or cache for change propagation.
wrong.B: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (DynamoDB Streams). The correct mechanism is required because dynamoDB Streams captures item-level changes and can feed downstream updates to another service database in near real time. Concept assessed: DynamoDB Streams. Exam trap: batch ETL or cache for change propagation.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Kinesis streams records; it is not a shared state/cache store. Concept assessed: DynamoDB Streams. Exam trap: batch ETL or cache for change propagation.

correct.D: Correta. DynamoDB Streams captura mudanças por item e alimenta atualizações downstream em outro banco quase em tempo real. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB Streams. Armadilha da prova: batch ETL or cache for change propagation. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/Streams.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Glue é ETL batch, não propagação quase real-time. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB Streams. Armadilha da prova: batch ETL or cache for change propagation.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (DynamoDB Streams). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque dynamoDB Streams captura mudanças por item e alimenta atualizações downstream em outro banco quase em tempo real. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB Streams. Armadilha da prova: batch ETL or cache for change propagation.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Kinesis transmite registros; não é cache/estado compartilhado. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB Streams. Armadilha da prova: batch ETL or cache for change propagation.

question.en: A company needs a fully-managed source control service that will work in AWS. The service must ensure that revision control synchronizes multiple distributed repositories by exchanging sets of changes peer-to-peer. All users need to work productively even when not connected to a network. Which source control service should be used?

O serviço deve garantir que o controle de revisão sincronize múltiplos repositórios distribuídos trocando conjuntos de alterações peer-to-peer. Todos os usuários precisam trabalhar produtivamente mesmo quando não estiverem conectados a uma rede. Qual serviço de controle de código-fonte deve ser usado?

en: Subversion.

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (CodeCommit/Git). wrong.B: Incorreta. CodeBuild compila/testa, não implanta ambientes ordenados. Conceito avaliado: CodeCommit/Git. Armadilha da prova: confusing build/project tools with source control.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (CodeCommit/Git). question.en: A Developer is writing a serverless application that requires that an AWS Lambda function be invoked every 10 minutes. What is an automated and serverless way to trigger the function?
question.pt: Um Developer está criando uma aplicação serverless que requer que uma função AWS Lambda seja invocada a cada 10 minutos. Qual é uma forma automatizada e serverless de acionar a função?

en: Deploy an Amazon EC2 instance based on Linux, and edit its `/etc/crontab` file by adding a command to periodically invoke the Lambda function.
Configure an environment variable named PERIOD for the Lambda function. Set the value to `600`.
Create an Amazon CloudWatch Events rule that triggers on a regular schedule to invoke the Lambda function.
Create an Amazon SNS topic that has a subscription to the Lambda function with a 600-second timer.
pt: Implantar uma instância Amazon EC2 baseada em Linux e editar seu arquivo `/etc/crontab` adicionando um comando para invocar periodicamente a função Lambda.
Configurar uma variável de ambiente chamada PERIOD para a função Lambda e definir o valor como `600`.
Criar uma regra do Amazon CloudWatch Events que é acionada em um horário regular para invocar a função Lambda.
Criar um tópico Amazon SNS com uma assinatura para a função Lambda com um timer de 600 segundos.

correct.C: Correct. A scheduled CloudWatch Events/EventBridge rule invokes Lambda periodically without servers. Concept assessed: scheduled Lambda. Exam trap: using EC2 cron or SNS timers. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eventbridge/latest/userguide/eb-create-rule-schedule.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. EC2 adds server management and is unnecessary for this managed/serverless requirement. Concept assessed: scheduled Lambda. Exam trap: using EC2 cron or SNS timers.

wrong.D: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (scheduled Lambda). Exam trap: using EC2 cron or SNS timers.

correct.C: Correta. Uma regra agendada CloudWatch Events/EventBridge invoca Lambda periodicamente sem servidores. Conceito avaliado: scheduled Lambda. Armadilha da prova: using EC2 cron or SNS timers. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eventbridge/latest/userguide/eb-create-rule-schedule.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. EC2 adiciona servidores e é desnecessário para esse requisito gerenciado/serverless. Conceito avaliado: scheduled Lambda. Armadilha da prova: using EC2 cron or SNS timers.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (scheduled Lambda). wrong.D: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (scheduled Lambda). question.en: A company is building an application to track athlete performance using an Amazon DynamoDB table. Each item in the table is identified by a partition key (`user_id`) and a sort key (`sport_name`). The table design is shown below. (Note: Not all table attributes are shown) A Developer is asked to write a leaderboard application to display the top performers (`user_id`) based on the score for each `sport_name`. What process will allow the Developer to extract results MOST efficiently from the DynamoDB table?
question.pt: Uma empresa está criando uma aplicação para rastrear o desempenho de atletas usando uma tabela Amazon DynamoDB. Cada item na tabela é identificado por uma partition key (`user_id`) e uma sort key (`sport_name`). O design da tabela é mostrado abaixo. Um Developer é solicitado a criar uma aplicação de leaderboard para exibir os melhores desempenhos (`user_id`) com base na pontuação para cada `sport_name`. Qual processo permitirá ao Developer extrair resultados de forma MAIS eficiente da tabela DynamoDB?

en: Use a DynamoDB query operation with the key attributes of `user_id` and `sport_name` and order the results based on the score attribute.
Create a global secondary index with a partition key of `sport_name` and a sort key of score, and get the results.
Use a DynamoDB scan operation to retrieve scores and `user_id` based on `sport_name`, and order the results based on the score attribute.
Create a local secondary index with a primary key of `sport_name` and a sort key of score and get the results based on the score attribute.
pt: Usar uma operação Query do DynamoDB com os atributos de chave `user_id` e `sport_name` e ordenar os resultados com base no atributo score.
Criar um índice secundário global com uma partition key de `sport_name` e uma sort key de score, e obter os resultados.
Usar uma operação Scan do DynamoDB para recuperar scores e `user_id` com base em `sport_name`, e ordenar os resultados com base no atributo score.
Criar um índice secundário local com uma primary key de `sport_name` e uma sort key de score e obter os resultados com base no atributo score.

correct.B: Correct. A GSI with sport_name as partition key and score as sort key supports efficient per-sport leaderboards ordered by score. Concept assessed: DynamoDB index design. Exam trap: querying/sorting non-key attributes. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/GSI.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (DynamoDB index design). The correct mechanism is required because a GSI with sport_name as partition key and score as sort key supports efficient per-sport leaderboards ordered by score. Concept assessed: DynamoDB index design. Exam trap: querying/sorting non-key attributes.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Scan reads broadly and costs more than Query. Concept assessed: DynamoDB index design. Exam trap: querying/sorting non-key attributes.
wrong.D: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (DynamoDB index design). The correct mechanism is required because a GSI with sport_name as partition key and score as sort key supports efficient per-sport leaderboards ordered by score. Concept assessed: DynamoDB index design. Exam trap: querying/sorting non-key attributes.

correct.B: Correta. Um GSI com sport_name como partition key e score como sort key suporta rankings eficientes por esporte ordenados por score. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB index design. Armadilha da prova: querying/sorting non-key attributes. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/GSI.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (DynamoDB index design). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque um GSI com sport_name como partition key e score como sort key suporta rankings eficientes por esporte ordenados por score. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB index design. Armadilha da prova: querying/sorting non-key attributes.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Scan lê amplamente e custa mais que Query. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB index design. Armadilha da prova: querying/sorting non-key attributes.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (DynamoDB index design). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque um GSI com sport_name como partition key e score como sort key suporta rankings eficientes por esporte ordenados por score. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB index design. Armadilha da prova: querying/sorting non-key attributes.

question.en: A Developer is creating a mobile application that will not require users to log in. What is the MOST efficient method to grant users access to AWS resources?
question.pt: Um Developer está criando uma aplicação mobile que não exigirá que os usuários façam login. Qual é o método MAIS eficiente para conceder aos usuários acesso aos recursos AWS?

en: Use an identity provider to securely authenticate with the application.
Create an AWS Lambda function to create an IAM user when a user accesses the application.
Create credentials using AWS KMS and apply these credentials to users when using the application.
Use Amazon Cognito to associate unauthenticated users with an IAM role that has limited access to resources.
pt: Usar um provedor de identidade para autenticar com segurança na aplicação.
Criar uma função AWS Lambda para criar um usuário IAM quando um usuário acessa a aplicação.
Criar credenciais usando o AWS KMS e aplicar essas credenciais aos usuários ao usar a aplicação.
Usar o Amazon Cognito para associar usuários não autenticados a uma função IAM com acesso limitado aos recursos.

correct.D: Correct. Cognito identity pools support unauthenticated identities and map them to limited IAM roles for guest AWS access. Concept assessed: Cognito guest access. Exam trap: creating IAM users for anonymous users. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cognito/latest/developerguide/identity-pools.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (Cognito guest access). The correct mechanism is required because cognito identity pools support unauthenticated identities and map them to limited IAM roles for guest AWS access. Concept assessed: Cognito guest access. Exam trap: creating IAM users for anonymous users.
wrong.B: Incorrect. Creating IAM users for app users does not scale and is unsafe. Concept assessed: Cognito guest access. Exam trap: creating IAM users for anonymous users.
wrong.C: Incorrect. KMS manages keys, not users/password reset/application directories. Concept assessed: Cognito guest access. Exam trap: creating IAM users for anonymous users.

correct.D: Correta. Cognito identity pools suportam identidades não autenticadas e as mapeiam para IAM roles limitadas. Conceito avaliado: Cognito guest access. Armadilha da prova: creating IAM users for anonymous users. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cognito/latest/developerguide/identity-pools.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (Cognito guest access). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque cognito identity pools suportam identidades não autenticadas e as mapeiam para IAM roles limitadas. Conceito avaliado: Cognito guest access. Armadilha da prova: creating IAM users for anonymous users.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Criar IAM users para usuários da app não escala e é inseguro. Conceito avaliado: Cognito guest access. Armadilha da prova: creating IAM users for anonymous users.
wrong.C: Incorreta. KMS gerencia chaves, não usuários/reset de senha. Conceito avaliado: Cognito guest access. Armadilha da prova: creating IAM users for anonymous users.

question.en: An application running on Amazon EC2 instances must access objects within an Amazon S3 bucket that are encrypted using server-side encryption using AWS KMS encryption keys (SSE-KMS). The application must have access to the customer master key (CMK) to decrypt the objects. Which combination of steps will grant the application access? (Select TWO)
question.pt: Uma aplicação executada em Amazon EC2 instâncias deve acessar objetos within an Amazon S3 bucket that are encrypted usando server-side encryption usando AWS KMS encryption keys (SSE-KMS). A aplicação deve have acessar para o customizadoer master key (CMK) para decrypt o objetos. Qual combinação de etapas will grant o application acessar? (Selecione DUAS)

en: Write an S3 bucket policy that grants the bucket access to the key.
Grant access to the key in the IAM EC2 role attached to the application's EC2 instances.
Write a key policy that enables IAM policies to grant access to the key.
Grant access to the key in the S3 bucket's ACL.
Create a Systems Manager parameter that exposes the KMS key to the EC2 instances.
pt: Write an S3 bucket policy that grants o bucket acessar para o key.
Grant acessar para o key em o IAM EC2 role attached para o application's EC2 instâncias.
Write a key policy that habilitars IAM policies para grant acessar para o key.
Grant acessar para o key em o S3 bucket's ACL.
Create a Systems Manager parameter that exposes o KMS key para o EC2 instâncias.

correct.B: Correct. SSE-KMS access requires both IAM permission for the EC2 role and a KMS key policy that allows those IAM permissions to be effective. Concept assessed: KMS authorization. Exam trap: granting only S3 access or ACLs. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/services-s3.html
correct.C: Correct. SSE-KMS access requires both IAM permission for the EC2 role and a KMS key policy that allows those IAM permissions to be effective. Concept assessed: KMS authorization. wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (KMS authorization). Concept assessed: KMS authorization. Exam trap: granting only S3 access or ACLs.
wrong.D: Incorrect. ACLs do not grant KMS decrypt permission. Concept assessed: KMS authorization. Exam trap: granting only S3 access or ACLs.
wrong.E: Incorrect. More memory does not fix repeated DB connection creation. Concept assessed: KMS authorization. Exam trap: granting only S3 access or ACLs.

correct.B: Correta. Acesso SSE-KMS exige permissão IAM para a role EC2 e key policy KMS que permita essas permissões. Conceito avaliado: KMS authorization. Armadilha da prova: granting only S3 access or ACLs. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/services-s3.html
correct.C: Correta. Acesso SSE-KMS exige permissão IAM para a role EC2 e key policy KMS que permita essas permissões. Conceito avaliado: KMS authorization. Armadilha da prova: granting only S3 access or ACLs. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/kms/latest/developerguide/services-s3.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (KMS authorization). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque acesso SSE-KMS exige permissão IAM para a role EC2 e key policy KMS que permita essas permissões. Conceito avaliado: KMS authorization. Armadilha da prova: granting only S3 access or ACLs.
wrong.D: Incorreta. ACLs não concedem KMS decrypt. Conceito avaliado: KMS authorization. Armadilha da prova: granting only S3 access or ACLs.
wrong.E: Incorreta. Mais memória não corrige criação repetida de conexão. Conceito avaliado: KMS authorization. Armadilha da prova: granting only S3 access or ACLs.

question.en: What does an Amazon SQS delay queue accomplish?
question.pt: O que uma fila de atraso (delay queue) do Amazon SQS realiza?

en: Messages are hidden for a configurable amount of time when they are first added to the queue.
Messages are hidden for a configurable amount of time after they are consumed from the queue.
The consumer can poll the queue for a configurable amount of time before retrieving a message.
Message cannot be deleted for a configurable amount of time after they are consumed from the queue.
pt: As mensagens ficam ocultas por um período configurável quando são adicionadas pela primeira vez à fila.
As mensagens ficam ocultas por um período configurável depois de serem consumidas da fila.
O consumidor pode consultar a fila por um período configurável antes de recuperar uma mensagem.
As mensagens não podem ser excluídas por um período configurável depois de serem consumidas da fila.

correct.A: Correct. A delay queue hides newly sent SQS messages for a configured delay before consumers can receive them. Concept assessed: SQS delay queue. Exam trap: confusing with visibility timeout. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/SQSDeveloperGuide/sqs-visibility-timeout.html

wrong.B: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (SQS delay queue). The correct mechanism is required because a delay queue hides newly sent SQS messages for a configured delay before consumers can receive them. Concept assessed: SQS delay queue. Exam trap: confusing with visibility timeout.

Exam trap: confusing with visibility timeout.
wrong.D: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (SQS delay queue). The correct mechanism is required because a delay queue hides newly sent SQS messages for a configured delay before consumers can receive them. Concept assessed: SQS delay queue. Exam trap: confusing with visibility timeout.

question.en: A company has multiple Developers located across the globe who are updating code incrementally for a development project. When Developers upload code concurrently, internet connectivity is slow and it is taking a long time to upload code for deployment in AWS Elastic Beanstalk. Which step will result in minimized upload and deployment time with the LEAST amount of administrative effort?
question.pt: Uma empresa tem múltiplos Developers localizados em todo o mundo que atualizam código incrementalmente para um projeto de desenvolvimento. Quando os Developers fazem upload de código simultaneamente, a conectividade com a internet é lenta e está demorando muito para fazer upload do código para implantação no AWS Elastic Beanstalk. Qual etapa resultará no menor tempo de upload e implantação com o MENOR esforço administrativo?

en: Allow the Developers to upload the code to an Amazon S3 bucket, and deploy it directly to Elastic Beanstalk.
Allow the Developers to upload the code to a central FTP server to deploy the application to Elastic Beanstalk.
Create an AWS CodeCommit repository, allow the Developers to commit code to it, and then directly deploy the code to Elastic Beanstalk.
Create a code repository on an Amazon EC2 instance so that all Developers can update the code, and deploy the application from the instance to Elastic Beanstalk.
pt: Permitir que os Developers façam upload do código para um bucket Amazon S3 e implantá-lo diretamente no Elastic Beanstalk.
Permitir que os Developers façam upload do código para um servidor FTP central para implantar a aplicação no Elastic Beanstalk.
Criar um repositório AWS CodeCommit, permitir que os Developers façam commit do código nele e depois implantar diretamente o código no Elastic Beanstalk.
Criar um repositório de código em uma instância Amazon EC2 para que todos os Developers possam atualizar o código e implantar a aplicação a partir da instância para o Elastic Beanstalk.

correct.C: Correct. CodeCommit lets distributed developers push incremental Git changes to a managed repository and deploy from there to Elastic Beanstalk. Concept assessed: CodeCommit deployment source. Exam trap: using FTP/EC2/S3 full uploads. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/using-features.rolling-version-deploy.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Beanstalk does not deploy serverless API Swagger definitions. Concept assessed: CodeCommit deployment source. Exam trap: using FTP/EC2/S3 full uploads.
wrong.B: Incorrect. FTP is unmanaged and not Git-based incremental source control. Concept assessed: CodeCommit deployment source. Exam trap: using FTP/EC2/S3 full uploads.
wrong.D: Incorrect. EC2 adds server management and is unnecessary for this managed/serverless requirement. Concept assessed: CodeCommit deployment source. Exam trap: using FTP/EC2/S3 full uploads.

correct.C: Correta. CodeCommit permite que developers distribuídos enviem mudanças incrementais Git a um repositório gerenciado e implantem no Beanstalk. Conceito avaliado: CodeCommit deployment source. Armadilha da prova: using FTP/EC2/S3 full uploads. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/using-features.rolling-version-deploy.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Beanstalk não implanta Swagger serverless. Conceito avaliado: CodeCommit deployment source. Armadilha da prova: using FTP/EC2/S3 full uploads.
wrong.B: Incorreta. FTP não é gerenciado nem controle incremental Git. Conceito avaliado: CodeCommit deployment source. Armadilha da prova: using FTP/EC2/S3 full uploads.
wrong.D: Incorreta. EC2 adiciona servidores e é desnecessário para esse requisito gerenciado/serverless. Conceito avaliado: CodeCommit deployment source. Armadilha da prova: using FTP/EC2/S3 full uploads.

question.en: A company recently migrated its web, application and NoSQL database tiers to AWS. The company is using Auto Scaling to scale the web and application tiers. More than 95 percent of the Amazon DynamoDB requests are repeated read requests. How can the DynamoDB NoSQL tier be scaled up to cache these repeated requests?
question.pt: Uma empresa migrou recentemente suas camadas web, de aplicação e de banco de dados NoSQL para a AWS. A empresa está usando Auto Scaling para escalar as camadas web e de aplicação. Mais de 95% das requisições ao Amazon DynamoDB são requisições de leitura repetidas. Como a camada NoSQL do DynamoDB pode ser escalada para armazenar em cache essas requisições repetidas?

en: Amazon EMR.
Amazon DynamoDB Accelerator.
Amazon SQS.
Amazon CloudFront.
pt: Amazon EMR.
Amazon DynamoDB Accelerator.
Amazon SQS.
Amazon CloudFront.

correct.B: Correct. DAX is an in-memory cache purpose-built for DynamoDB repeated reads and low latency. Concept assessed: DynamoDB DAX. Exam trap: using EMR/SQS/CloudFront for DB reads. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. EMR is for big data processing, not caching repeated reads. Concept assessed: DynamoDB DAX. Exam trap: using EMR/SQS/CloudFront for DB reads.
wrong.C: Incorrect. SQS queues messages; it does not manage REST APIs. Concept assessed: DynamoDB DAX. Exam trap: using EMR/SQS/CloudFront for DB reads.
wrong.D: Incorrect. CloudFront caches HTTP content, not direct RDS query results or DynamoDB API reads. Concept assessed: DynamoDB DAX. Exam trap: using EMR/SQS/CloudFront for DB reads.

correct.B: Correta. DAX é cache em memória específico para leituras repetidas do DynamoDB com baixa latência. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB DAX. Armadilha da prova: using EMR/SQS/CloudFront for DB reads. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. EMR é big data, não cache de leituras. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB DAX. Armadilha da prova: using EMR/SQS/CloudFront for DB reads.
wrong.C: Incorreta. SQS enfileira mensagens; não gerencia APIs REST. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB DAX. Armadilha da prova: using EMR/SQS/CloudFront for DB reads.
wrong.D: Incorreta. CloudFront cacheia HTTP, não consultas RDS ou leituras DynamoDB diretas. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB DAX. Armadilha da prova: using EMR/SQS/CloudFront for DB reads.

Permissions associated with the account that owns the AWS service.

question.en: An organization is storing large files in Amazon S3, and is writing a web application to display meta-data about the files to end-users. Based on the metadata a user selects an object to download. The organization needs a mechanism to index the files and provide single-digit millisecond latency retrieval for the metadata. What AWS service should be used to accomplish this?
question.pt: An organization is storing large arquivos em Amazon S3, e is writing a web application para display meta-dados about o arquivos para end-usuários. Based em o metadados a user selects an objeto para download. O organization needs a mechanism para index o arquivos e provide single-digit millisecond latência retrieval para o metadados. What AWS service should be used para accomplish this?

en: Amazon DynamoDB.
Amazon EC2.
AWS Lambda.
Amazon RDS.
pt: Amazon DynamoDB.
Amazon EC2.
AWS Lambda.
Amazon RDS.

correct.A: Correct. DynamoDB provides indexed metadata lookup with single-digit millisecond latency while S3 stores the large files. Concept assessed: DynamoDB metadata index. Exam trap: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-memory.html

wrong.B: Incorrect. EC2 adds server management and is unnecessary for this managed/serverless requirement. Concept assessed: DynamoDB metadata index. Exam trap: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.
wrong.C: Incorrect. Lambda runs code but is not an API management facade. Concept assessed: DynamoDB metadata index. Exam trap: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.
wrong.D: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: DynamoDB metadata index. Exam trap: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.

correct.A: Correta. DynamoDB fornece consulta indexada de metadados com milissegundos de um dígito enquanto S3 armazena arquivos grandes. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB metadata index. Armadilha da prova: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/welcome.html

wrong.B: Incorreta. EC2 adiciona servidores e é desnecessário para esse requisito gerenciado/serverless. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB metadata index. Armadilha da prova: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Lambda executa código, mas não é fachada de gerenciamento de API. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB metadata index. Armadilha da prova: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.
wrong.D: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: DynamoDB metadata index. Armadilha da prova: using compute/relational/search unnecessarily.

question.en: A stock market monitoring application uses Amazon Kinesis for data ingestion. During simulated tests of peak data rates, the Kinesis stream cannot keep up with the incoming data. What step will allow Kinesis to accommodate the traffic during peak hours?
question.pt: A stock market monitoring application usa Amazon Kinesis para dados ingestion. During simulated tests of peak dados rates, o Kinesis stream cannot keep up com o incoming dados. What step will permitir Kinesis para accommodate o traffic during peak hours?

en: Install the Kinesis Producer Library (KPL) for ingesting data into the stream.
Reduce the data retention period to allow for more data ingestion using `DecreaseStreamRetentionPeriod`.
Increase the shard count of the stream using `UpdateShardCount`.
Ingest multiple records into the stream in a single call using `PutRecords`.
pt: Install o Kinesis Producer Library (KPL) para ingesting dados into o stream.
Reduce o dados retention period para permitir para more dados ingestion usando `DecreaseStreamRetentionPeriod`.
Increase o shard count of o stream usando `UpdateShardCount`.
Ingest multiple records into o stream em a single call usando `PutRecords`.

correct.C: Correct. Kinesis Data Streams throughput scales by shard count; `UpdateShardCount` increases capacity for peak ingestion. Concept assessed: Kinesis shards. Exam trap: optimizing producer calls when shards are saturated. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Kinesis streams records; it is not a shared state/cache store. Concept assessed: Kinesis shards. Exam trap: optimizing producer calls when shards are saturated.
wrong.B: Incorrect. Retention does not increase ingestion throughput. Concept assessed: Kinesis shards. Exam trap: optimizing producer calls when shards are saturated.
wrong.D: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: Kinesis shards. Exam trap: optimizing producer calls when shards are saturated.

correct.C: Correta. Throughput do Kinesis Data Streams escala pela quantidade de shards; `UpdateShardCount` aumenta capacidade para picos. Conceito avaliado: Kinesis shards. Armadilha da prova: optimizing producer calls when shards are saturated. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Kinesis transmite registros; não é cache/estado compartilhado. Conceito avaliado: Kinesis shards. Armadilha da prova: optimizing producer calls when shards are saturated.

en: Write a script that deletes old records; schedule the scripts as a cron job on an Amazon EC2 instance.

question.en: An application is expected to process many files. Each file takes four minutes to process each AWS Lambda invocation. The Lambda function does not return any important data. What is the fastest way to process all the files?

en: First split the files to make them smaller, then process with synchronous RequestResponse Lambda invocations.
Make synchronous RequestResponse Lambda invocations and process the files one by one.
Make asynchronous Event Lambda invocations and process the files in parallel.

correct.C: Correct. Asynchronous Event invocations let independent Lambda executions process files in parallel without waiting for return data. Concept assessed: Lambda async parallelism. Exam trap: serial synchronous processing. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/invocation-async.html

question.en: A company needs a new REST API that can return information about the contents of an Amazon S3 bucket, such as a count of the objects stored in it. The company has decided that the new API should be written as a microservice using AWS Lambda and Amazon API Gateway. How should the Developer ensure that the microservice has the necessary access to the Amazon S3 bucket, while adhering to security best practices?
question.pt: Uma empresa precisa de uma nova API REST que possa retornar informações sobre o conteúdo de um bucket Amazon S3, como a contagem de objetos armazenados nele. A empresa decidiu que a nova API deve ser escrita como um microsserviço usando AWS Lambda e Amazon API Gateway. Como o Developer deve garantir que o microsserviço tenha o acesso necessário ao bucket Amazon S3, seguindo as melhores práticas de segurança?

en: Create an IAM user that has permissions to access the Amazon S3 bucket, and store the IAM user credentials in the Lambda function source code.
Create an IAM role that has permissions to access the Amazon S3 bucket and assign it to the Lambda function as its execution role.
Create an Amazon S3 bucket policy that specifies the Lambda service as its principal and assign it to the Amazon S3 bucket.
Create an IAM role, attach the AmazonS3FullAccess managed policy to it, and assign the role to the Lambda function as its execution role.
pt: Criar um usuário IAM com permissões para acessar o bucket Amazon S3 e armazenar as credenciais do usuário IAM no código-fonte da função Lambda.
Criar uma função IAM com permissões para acessar o bucket Amazon S3 e atribuí-la à função Lambda como sua execution role.
Criar uma política de bucket do Amazon S3 que especifique o serviço Lambda como seu principal e atribuí-la ao bucket Amazon S3.
Criar uma função IAM, anexar a política gerenciada AmazonS3FullAccess a ela e atribuir a função à função Lambda como sua execution role.

correct.B: Correct. A Lambda execution role with least-privilege S3 permissions provides temporary credentials without storing IAM user keys in code. Concept assessed: Lambda execution role. Exam trap: embedded keys or overbroad access. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-function-common.html#configuration-function-folders

wrong.A: Incorrect. Creating IAM users for app users does not scale and is unsafe. Concept assessed: Lambda execution role. Exam trap: embedded keys or overbroad access.
wrong.C: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (Lambda execution role). The correct mechanism is required because a Lambda execution role with least-privilege S3 permissions provides temporary credentials without storing IAM user keys in code. Concept assessed: Lambda execution role. Exam trap: embedded keys or overbroad access.
wrong.D: Incorrect. FullAccess is broader than least privilege. Concept assessed: Lambda execution role. Exam trap: embedded keys or overbroad access.

correct.B: Correta. Uma execution role da Lambda com permissões S3 mínimas fornece credenciais temporárias sem armazenar chaves no código. Conceito avaliado: Lambda execution role. Armadilha da prova: embedded keys or overbroad access. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-function-common.html#configuration-function-folders

wrong.A: Incorreta. Criar IAM users para usuários da app não escala e é inseguro. Conceito avaliado: Lambda execution role. Armadilha da prova: embedded keys or overbroad access.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (Lambda execution role). wrong.D: Incorreta. FullAccess é mais amplo que least privilege. Conceito avaliado: Lambda execution role. Armadilha da prova: embedded keys or overbroad access.

question.en: An organization is using Amazon CloudFront to ensure that its users experience low-latency access to its web application. The organization has identified a need to encrypt all traffic between users and CloudFront, and all traffic between CloudFront and the web application. How can these requirements be met? (Choose TWO)
question.pt: Uma organização está usando o Amazon CloudFront para garantir que seus usuários tenham acesso de baixa latência à sua aplicação web. A organização identificou a necessidade de criptografar todo o tráfego entre os usuários e o CloudFront, e todo o tráfego entre o CloudFront e a aplicação web. Como esses requisitos podem ser atendidos? (Escolha DUAS)

en: Use AWS KMS to encrypt traffic between CloudFront and the web application.
Set the Origin Protocol Policy to `HTTPS Only`.
Set the Origin's HTTP Port to `443`.
Set the Viewer Protocol Policy to `HTTPS Only` or `Redirect HTTP to HTTPS`.
Enable the CloudFront option `Restrict Viewer Access`.
pt: Usar o AWS KMS para criptografar o tráfego entre o CloudFront e a aplicação web.
Definir a Origin Protocol Policy como `HTTPS Only`.
Definir a HTTP Port da origem como `443`.
Definir a Viewer Protocol Policy como `HTTPS Only` ou `Redirect HTTP to HTTPS`.
Habilitar a opção do CloudFront `Restrict Viewer Access`.

correct.B: Correct. Viewer Protocol Policy enforces HTTPS from users to CloudFront; Origin Protocol Policy HTTPS Only enforces HTTPS from CloudFront to the origin. Concept assessed: CloudFront TLS. Exam trap: confusing KMS/signed URLs with transport encryption. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html
correct.D: Correct. Viewer Protocol Policy enforces HTTPS from users to CloudFront; Origin Protocol Policy HTTPS Only enforces HTTPS from CloudFront to the origin. Concept assessed: CloudFront TLS. Exam trap: confusing KMS/signed URLs with transport encryption. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. KMS manages keys, not users/password reset/application directories. Concept assessed: CloudFront TLS. Exam trap: confusing KMS/signed URLs with transport encryption.

question.en: An application needs to use the IP address of the client in its processing. The application has been moved into AWS and has been placed behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB). However, all the client IP addresses now appear to be the same. The application must maintain the ability to scale horizontally. Based on this scenario, what is the MOST cost-effective solution to this problem?
question.pt: Uma aplicação precisa usar o endereço IP do cliente em seu processamento. A aplicação foi migrada para a AWS e colocada atrás de um Application Load Balancer (ALB). No entanto, todos os endereços IP dos clientes agora parecem ser os mesmos. A aplicação deve manter a capacidade de escalar horizontalmente. Com base nesse cenário, qual é a solução MAIS econômica para esse problema?

en: Remove the application from the ALB. Delete the ALB and change Amazon Route 53 to direct traffic to the instance running the application.
Remove the application from the ALB. Create a Classic Load Balancer in its place. Direct traffic to the application using the HTTP protocol.

question.en: A company is using AWS CodePipeline to deliver one of its applications. The delivery pipeline is triggered by changes to the master branch of an AWS CodeCommit repository and uses AWS CodeBuild to implement the test and build stages of the process and AWS CodeDeploy to deploy the application. The pipeline has been operating successfully for several months and there have been no modifications. Following a recent change to the application's source code, AWS CodeDeploy has not deployed the updates application as expected. What are the possible causes? (Choose TWO)
question.pt: Uma empresa está usando o AWS CodePipeline para entregar uma de suas aplicações. O pipeline de entrega é acionado por alterações no branch master de um repositório AWS CodeCommit e usa o AWS CodeBuild para implementar as etapas de teste e build do processo, e o AWS CodeDeploy para implantar a aplicação. O pipeline tem operado com sucesso por vários meses e não houve modificações. Após uma alteração recente no código-fonte da aplicação, o AWS CodeDeploy não implantou a aplicação atualizada conforme esperado. Quais são as causas possíveis? (Escolha DUAS)

en: The change was not made in the master branch of the AWS CodeCommit repository.
One of the earlier stages in the pipeline failed and the pipeline has terminated.
One of the Amazon EC2 instances in the company's AWS CodePipeline cluster is inactive.
The AWS CodePipeline is incorrectly configured and is not executing AWS CodeDeploy.
AWS CodePipeline does not have permissions to access AWS CodeCommit.
pt: A alteração não foi feita no branch master do repositório AWS CodeCommit.
Um dos estágios anteriores no pipeline falhou e o pipeline foi encerrado.
Uma das instâncias Amazon EC2 no cluster AWS CodePipeline da empresa está inativa.
O AWS CodePipeline está incorretamente configurado e não está executando o AWS CodeDeploy.
O AWS CodePipeline não tem permissões para acessar o AWS CodeCommit.

correct.A: Correct. If the change is not on the monitored master branch, the pipeline may not trigger; if an earlier stage fails, CodeDeploy is not reached. Concept assessed: CodePipeline flow. correct.B: Correct. If the change is not on the monitored master branch, the pipeline may not trigger; if an earlier stage fails, CodeDeploy is not reached. Concept assessed: CodePipeline flow. Exam trap: blaming unchanged config. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/codepipeline/latest/userguide/troubleshooting.html

wrong.C: Incorrect. EC2 adds server management and is unnecessary for this managed/serverless requirement. Concept assessed: CodePipeline flow. Exam trap: blaming unchanged config.
wrong.D: Incorrect. CodeDeploy deploys revisions; it does not create APIs from Swagger. Concept assessed: CodePipeline flow. Exam trap: blaming unchanged config.
wrong.E: Incorrect. CodeCommit is source control, not deployment orchestration. Concept assessed: CodePipeline flow. Exam trap: blaming unchanged config.

correct.B: Correta. Se a mudança não está no branch master monitorado, a pipeline pode não disparar; se etapa anterior falha, CodeDeploy não é alcançado. Conceito avaliado: CodePipeline flow. wrong.D: Incorreta. CodeDeploy implanta revisões; não cria API do Swagger. Conceito avaliado: CodePipeline flow. Armadilha da prova: blaming unchanged config.
wrong.E: Incorreta. CodeCommit é controle de fonte, não orquestra implantação. Conceito avaliado: CodePipeline flow. Armadilha da prova: blaming unchanged config.

question.en: The Developer for a retail company must integrate a fraud detection solution into the order processing solution. The fraud detection solution takes between ten and thirty minutes to verify an order. At peak, the web site can receive one hundred orders per minute. What is the most scalable method to add the fraud detection solution to the order processing pipeline?
question.pt: Uma solução de detecção de fraude leva de 10 a 30 minutos por pedido, e o site recebe até 100 pedidos por minuto. Qual é o método mais escalável para integrar a fraude ao pipeline de pedidos?

en: Add all new orders to an Amazon SQS queue. Configure a fleet of 10 EC2 instances spanning multiple AZs with the fraud detection solution installed on them to pull orders from this queue. Update the order with a pass or fails status.

Add all new orders to an SQS queue. Configure an Auto Scaling group that uses the queue depth metric as its unit of scale to launch a dynamically-sized fleet of EC2 instances spanning multiple AZs with the fraud detection solution installed on them to pull orders from this queue. Update the order with a pass or fails status.

Add all new orders to an Amazon Kinesis Stream. Subscribe a Lambda function to automatically read batches of records from the Kinesis Stream. The Lambda function includes the fraud detection software and will update the order with a pass or fail status.

Write all new orders to Amazon DynamoDB. Configure DynamoDB Streams to include all new orders. Subscribe a Lambda function to automatically read batches of records from the Kinesis Stream. The Lambda function includes the fraud detection software and will update the order with a pass or fail status.
pt: Add all new orders para an Amazon SQS fila. Configure a fleet of 10 EC2 instâncias spanning multiple AZs com o fraud detection solution installed em them para pull orders de this fila. Update o order com a pass ou fails status.

Add all new orders para an SQS fila. Configure an Auto Scaling group that usa o fila depth métrica como its unit of scale para launch a dynamically-sized fleet of EC2 instâncias spanning multiple AZs com o fraud detection solution installed em them para pull orders de this fila. Update o order com a pass ou fails status.

Add all new orders para an Amazon Kinesis Stream. Subscribe a Lambda função para automatically ler batches of records de o Kinesis Stream. O Lambda função includes o fraud detection software e will update o order com a pass ou fail status.

Write all new orders para Amazon DynamoDB. Configure DynamoDB Streams para include all new orders. Subscribe a Lambda função para automatically ler batches of records de o Kinesis Stream. O Lambda função includes o fraud detection software e will update o order com a pass ou fail status.

correct.B: Correct. SQS decouples order intake; Auto Scaling workers based on queue depth scale dynamically for long fraud checks across AZs. Concept assessed: queue-based scaling. Exam trap: fixed fleet or Lambda for 10-30 minute jobs. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-memory.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. SQS queues messages; it does not manage REST APIs. Concept assessed: queue-based scaling. Exam trap: fixed fleet or Lambda for 10-30 minute jobs.
wrong.C: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: queue-based scaling. Exam trap: fixed fleet or Lambda for 10-30 minute jobs.
wrong.D: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: queue-based scaling. Exam trap: fixed fleet or Lambda for 10-30 minute jobs.

correct.B: Correta. SQS desacopla entrada de pedidos; workers com Auto Scaling baseado na profundidade da fila escalam dinamicamente para verificações longas. Conceito avaliado: queue-based scaling. Armadilha da prova: fixed fleet or Lambda for 10-30 minute jobs. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/welcome.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. SQS enfileira mensagens; não gerencia APIs REST. Conceito avaliado: queue-based scaling. Armadilha da prova: fixed fleet or Lambda for 10-30 minute jobs.
wrong.C: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: queue-based scaling. Armadilha da prova: fixed fleet or Lambda for 10-30 minute jobs.
wrong.D: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: queue-based scaling. Armadilha da prova: fixed fleet or Lambda for 10-30 minute jobs.

question.en: When a Developer tries to run an AWS CodeBuild project, it raises an error because the length of all environment variables exceeds the limit for the combined maximum of characters. What is the recommended solution?

en: Add the export `LC_ALL="en_US.utf8"` command to the `pre_build` section to ensure `POSIX` localization.
Use Amazon Cognito to store key-value pairs for large numbers of environment variables.
Update the settings for the build project to use an Amazon S3 bucket for large numbers of environment variables.
Use AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store to store large numbers of environment variables.

Usar o AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store para armazenar um grande número de variáveis de ambiente.

question.en: A set of APIs are exposed to customers using the Amazon API Gateway. These APIs have caching enabled on the API Gateway. Customers have asked for an option to invalidate this cache for each of the APIs. What action can be taken to allow API customers to invalidate the API Cache?
question.pt: Um conjunto de APIs está exposto para clientes usando o Amazon API Gateway. Essas APIs têm caching habilitado no API Gateway. Os clientes solicitaram uma opção para invalidar esse cache para cada uma das APIs. Qual ação pode ser tomada para permitir que os clientes da API invalidem o cache da API?

en: Ask customers to use AWS credentials to call the `InvalidateCache` API.
Ask customers to invoke an AWS API endpoint which invalidates the cache.
Ask customers to pass an HTTP header called `Cache-Control:max-age=0`.
Ask customers to add a query string parameter called `INVALIDATE_CACHE` when making an API call.
pt: Pedir aos clientes que usem credenciais AWS para chamar a API `InvalidateCache`.
Pedir aos clientes que invoquem um endpoint de API AWS que invalida o cache.
Pedir aos clientes que passem um cabeçalho HTTP chamado `Cache-Control:max-age=0`.
Pedir aos clientes que adicionem um parâmetro de query string chamado `INVALIDATE_CACHE` ao fazer uma chamada de API.

correct.C: Correct. API Gateway cache can be invalidated by clients with `Cache-Control:max-age=0` when the stage permits it. Concept assessed: API Gateway cache. Exam trap: invented query/API credential approaches. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/Strategies.html

question.en: A Developer has been asked to build a real-time dashboard web application to visualize the key prefixes and storage size of objects in Amazon S3 buckets. Amazon DynamoDB will be used to store the Amazon S3 metadata. What is the optimal and MOST cost-effective design to ensure that the real-time dashboard is kept up to date with the state of the objects in the Amazon S3 buckets?
question.pt: Um Developer has been asked para build a real-time dashboard web application para visualize o key prefixes e storage size of objetos em Amazon S3 buckets. Amazon DynamoDB will be used para armazenar o Amazon S3 metadados. What is o optimal e MOST econômica design para ensure that o real-time dashboard is kept up para date com o state of o objetos em o Amazon S3 buckets?

en: Use an Amazon CloudWatch event backed by an AWS Lambda function. Issue an Amazon S3 API call to get a list of all Amazon S3 objects and persist the metadata within DynamoDB. Have the web application poll the DynamoDB table to reflect this change.
Use Amazon S3 Event Notification backed by a Lambda function to persist the metadata into DynamoDB. Have the web application poll the DynamoDB table to reflect this change.
Run a cron job within an Amazon EC2 instance to list all objects within Amazon S3 and persist the metadata into DynamoDB. Have the web application poll the DynamoDB table to reflect this change.
Create a new Amazon EMR cluster to get all the metadata about Amazon S3 objects; persist the metadata into DynamoDB. Have the web application poll the DynamoDB table to reflect this change.
pt: Use an Amazon CloudWatch event backed por an AWS Lambda função. Issue an Amazon S3 API call para get a list of all Amazon S3 objetos e persist o metadados within DynamoDB. Have o web application poll o DynamoDB tabela para reflect this change.
Use Amazon S3 Event Notification backed por a Lambda função para persist o metadados into DynamoDB. Have o web application poll o DynamoDB tabela para reflect this change.
Run a cron job within an Amazon EC2 instância para list all objetos within Amazon S3 e persist o metadados into DynamoDB. Have o web application poll o DynamoDB tabela para reflect this change.
Create a new Amazon EMR cluster para get all o metadados about Amazon S3 objetos; persist o metadados into DynamoDB. Have o web application poll o DynamoDB tabela para reflect this change.

correct.B: Correct. S3 Event Notifications trigger Lambda on object changes, letting Lambda update DynamoDB metadata incrementally and cheaply. Concept assessed: S3 event-driven metadata. Exam trap: polling/listing all objects. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/optimizing-performance.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (S3 event-driven metadata). The correct mechanism is required because s3 Event Notifications trigger Lambda on object changes, letting Lambda update DynamoDB metadata incrementally and cheaply. Concept assessed: S3 event-driven metadata. Exam trap: polling/listing all objects.
wrong.C: Incorrect. EC2 adds server management and is unnecessary for this managed/serverless requirement. Concept assessed: S3 event-driven metadata. Exam trap: polling/listing all objects.
wrong.D: Incorrect. EMR is for big data processing, not caching repeated reads. Concept assessed: S3 event-driven metadata. Exam trap: polling/listing all objects.

correct.B: Correta. S3 Event Notifications acionam Lambda em mudanças de objeto, permitindo atualizar metadados no DynamoDB de forma incremental e barata. Conceito avaliado: S3 event-driven metadata. Armadilha da prova: polling/listing all objects. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/optimizing-performance.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (S3 event-driven metadata). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque s3 Event Notifications acionam Lambda em mudanças de objeto, permitindo atualizar metadados no DynamoDB de forma incremental e barata. Conceito avaliado: S3 event-driven metadata. Armadilha da prova: polling/listing all objects.
wrong.C: Incorreta. EC2 adiciona servidores e é desnecessário para esse requisito gerenciado/serverless. Conceito avaliado: S3 event-driven metadata. Armadilha da prova: polling/listing all objects.
wrong.D: Incorreta. EMR é big data, não cache de leituras. Conceito avaliado: S3 event-driven metadata. Armadilha da prova: polling/listing all objects.

question.en: A Developer must repeatedly and consistently deploy a serverless RESTful API on AWS. Which techniques will work? (Choose TWO)
question.pt: Um Developer deve implantar repetidamente e de forma consistente uma API RESTful serverless na AWS. Quais técnicas funcionam? (Escolha DUAS)

en: Define a Swagger file. Use AWS Elastic Beanstalk to deploy the Swagger file.
Define a Swagger file. Use AWS CodeDeploy to deploy the Swagger file.
Deploy a SAM template with an inline Swagger definition.
Define a Swagger file. Deploy a SAM template that references the Swagger file.
Define an inline Swagger definition in a Lambda function. Invoke the Lambda function.
pt: Define a Swagger arquivo. Use AWS Elastic Beanstalk para implantar o Swagger arquivo.
Define a Swagger arquivo. Use AWS CodeDeploy para implantar o Swagger arquivo.

Define a Swagger arquivo. Deploy a SAM template that references o Swagger arquivo.
Define an inline Swagger definition em a Lambda função. Invoke o Lambda função.

correct.C: Correct. SAM supports serverless APIs with inline Swagger/OpenAPI or a referenced Swagger file for repeatable deployments. Concept assessed: SAM API definitions. correct.D: Correct. SAM supports serverless APIs with inline Swagger/OpenAPI or a referenced Swagger file for repeatable deployments. Concept assessed: SAM API definitions. wrong.E: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (SAM API definitions). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque sAM suporta APIs serverless com Swagger/OpenAPI inline ou arquivo Swagger referenciado para implantações repetíveis. Conceito avaliado: SAM API definitions. Armadilha da prova: deploying Swagger with Beanstalk/CodeDeploy.

question.en: An existing serverless application processes uploaded image files. The process currently uses a single Lambda function that takes an image file, performs the processing, and stores the file in Amazon S3. Users of the application now require thumbnail generation of the images. Users want to avoid any impact to the time it takes to perform the image uploads. How can thumbnail generation be added to the application, meeting user requirements while minimizing changes to existing code?
question.pt: An existing serverless application processes uploaded image arquivos. O process currently usa a single Lambda função that takes an image arquivo, performs o processing, e armazenars o arquivo em Amazon S3. Usuários of o application now require thumbnail generation of o images. Usuários want para avoid any impact para o time it takes para perform o image uploads. How can thumbnail generation be added para o application, meeting user requirements while minimizing changes para existing código?

en: Change the existing Lambda function handling the uploads to create thumbnails at the time of upload. Have the function store both the image and thumbnail in Amazon S3.
Create a second Lambda function that handles thumbnail generation and storage. Change the existing Lambda function to invoke it asynchronously.
Create an S3 event notification with a Lambda function destination. Create a new Lambda function to generate and store thumbnails.
Create an S3 event notification to an SQS Queue. Create a scheduled Lambda function that processes the queue, and generates and stores thumbnails.
pt: Change o existing Lambda função handling o uploads para criar thumbnails at o time of upload. Have o função armazenar both o image e thumbnail em Amazon S3.
Create a second Lambda função that handles thumbnail generation e storage. Change o existing Lambda função para invoke it asynchronously.
Create an S3 event notification com a Lambda função destination. Create a new Lambda função para generate e armazenar thumbnails.
Create an S3 event notification para an SQS Queue. Create a scheduled Lambda função that processes o fila, e generates e armazenars thumbnails.

correct.C: Correct. An S3 event notification can trigger a separate Lambda after upload, generating thumbnails asynchronously without slowing the upload path. Concept assessed: S3 events to Lambda. Exam trap: doing thumbnail work in upload function. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/EventNotifications.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Doing thumbnails in upload Lambda increases upload latency. Concept assessed: S3 events to Lambda. Exam trap: doing thumbnail work in upload function.
wrong.B: Incorrect. Doing thumbnails in upload Lambda increases upload latency. Concept assessed: S3 events to Lambda. Exam trap: doing thumbnail work in upload function.
wrong.D: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (S3 events to Lambda). The correct mechanism is required because an S3 event notification can trigger a separate Lambda after upload, generating thumbnails asynchronously without slowing the upload path. Concept assessed: S3 events to Lambda. Exam trap: doing thumbnail work in upload function.

correct.C: Correta. Uma S3 event notification aciona uma Lambda separada após upload, gerando thumbnails assincronamente sem atrasar o upload. Conceito avaliado: S3 events to Lambda. Armadilha da prova: doing thumbnail work in upload function. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/EventNotifications.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Gerar thumbnail na Lambda de upload aumenta latência. Conceito avaliado: S3 events to Lambda. Armadilha da prova: doing thumbnail work in upload function.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Gerar thumbnail na Lambda de upload aumenta latência. Conceito avaliado: S3 events to Lambda. Armadilha da prova: doing thumbnail work in upload function.
wrong.D: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (S3 events to Lambda). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque uma S3 event notification aciona uma Lambda separada após upload, gerando thumbnails assincronamente sem atrasar o upload. Conceito avaliado: S3 events to Lambda. Armadilha da prova: doing thumbnail work in upload function.

question.en: A company is using Amazon API Gateway to manage access to a set of microservices implemented as AWS Lambda functions. Following a bug report, the company makes a minor breaking change to one of the APIs. In order to avoid impacting existing clients when the new API is deployed, the company wants to allow clients six months to migrate from v1 to v2. Which approach should the Developer use to handle this change?
question.pt: Uma empresa usa Amazon API Gateway para manage acessar para a set of microservices implemented como AWS Lambda funçãos. Following a bug report, o company makes a minor breaking change para one of o APIs. In order para avoid impacting existing clients when o new API is implantared, o company quer permitir clients six months para migrate de v1 para v2. Qual abordagem should o Developer use para handle this change?

en: Update the underlying Lambda function and provide clients with the new Lambda invocation URL.
Use API Gateway to automatically propagate the change to clients, specifying 180 days in the phased deployment parameter.
Use API Gateway to deploy a new stage named v2 to the API and provide users with its URL.
Update the underlying Lambda function, create an Amazon CloudFront distribution with the updated Lambda function as its origin.
pt: Update o underlying Lambda função e provide clients com o new Lambda invocation URL.
Use API Gateway para automatically propagate o change para clients, specifying 180 days em o phased implantarment parameter.
Use API Gateway para implantar a new stage named v2 para o API e provide usuários com its URL.
Update o underlying Lambda função, criar an Amazon CloudFront distribution com o updated Lambda função como its origin.

correct.C: Correct. API Gateway stages provide versioned endpoints such as v1 and v2 so old clients continue using v1 while migrating. Concept assessed: API versioning. Exam trap: expecting automatic migration. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Lambda URLs bypass API Gateway versioning. Concept assessed: API versioning. Exam trap: expecting automatic migration.
wrong.B: Incorrect. More memory does not fix repeated DB connection creation. Concept assessed: API versioning. Exam trap: expecting automatic migration.
wrong.D: Incorrect. CloudFront caches HTTP content, not direct RDS query results or DynamoDB API reads. Concept assessed: API versioning. Exam trap: expecting automatic migration.

correct.C: Correta. Stages do API Gateway fornecem endpoints versionados como v1 e v2 para clientes antigos continuarem no v1 durante migração. Conceito avaliado: API versioning. Armadilha da prova: expecting automatic migration. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonElastiCache/latest/dg/elasticache-use-cases.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Lambda URL contorna versionamento do API Gateway. Conceito avaliado: API versioning. Armadilha da prova: expecting automatic migration.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Mais memória não corrige criação repetida de conexão. Conceito avaliado: API versioning. Armadilha da prova: expecting automatic migration.
wrong.D: Incorreta. CloudFront cacheia HTTP, não consultas RDS ou leituras DynamoDB diretas. Conceito avaliado: API versioning. Armadilha da prova: expecting automatic migration.

question.en: A company developed a set of APIs that are being served through the Amazon API Gateway. The API calls need to be authenticated based on OpenID identity providers such as Amazon or Facebook. The APIs should allow access based on a custom authorization model. Which is the simplest and MOST secure design to use to build an authentication and authorization model for the APIs?
question.pt: Uma empresa desenvolveu um conjunto de APIs que estão sendo servidas pelo Amazon API Gateway. As chamadas de API precisam ser autenticadas com base em provedores de identidade OpenID, como Amazon ou Facebook. As APIs devem permitir acesso com base em um modelo de autorização personalizado. Qual é o design mais simples e MAIS seguro para criar um modelo de autenticação e autorização para as APIs?

en: Use Amazon Cognito user pools and a custom authorizer to authenticate and authorize users based on JSON Web Tokens.
Build a OpenID token broker with Amazon and Facebook. Users will authenticate with these identify providers and pass the JSON Web Token to the API to authenticate each API call.

Store user credentials in Amazon DynamoDB and have the application retrieve temporary credentials from AWS STS. Make API calls by passing user credentials to the APIs for authentication and authorization.
Use Amazon RDS to store user credentials and pass them to the APIs for authentications and authorization.
pt: Usar user pools do Amazon Cognito e um autorizador personalizado para autenticar e autorizar usuários com base em JSON Web Tokens.
Criar um broker de token OpenID com Amazon e Facebook. Os usuários se autenticarão com esses provedores de identidade e passarão o JSON Web Token para a API para autenticar cada chamada de API.

Armazenar credenciais de usuário no Amazon DynamoDB e fazer a aplicação recuperar credenciais temporárias do AWS STS. Fazer chamadas de API passando credenciais de usuário para as APIs para autenticação e autorização.
Usar o Amazon RDS para armazenar credenciais de usuário e passá-las para as APIs para autenticação e autorização.

correct.A: Correct. Cognito user pools federate OpenID providers and issue JWTs; a custom authorizer validates tokens and applies custom authorization. Concept assessed: API auth. Exam trap: building credential stores manually. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/apigateway/latest/developerguide/apigateway-use-lambda-authorizer.html

wrong.B: Incorrect. A custom broker adds security and maintenance risk. Concept assessed: API auth. Exam trap: building credential stores manually.

The correct mechanism is required because cognito user pools federate OpenID providers and issue JWTs; a custom authorizer validates tokens and applies custom authorization. Concept assessed: API auth. Exam trap: building credential stores manually.
wrong.D: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: API auth. Exam trap: building credential stores manually.
