question.en: Where should an Elastic Beanstalk configuration file named `healthcheckur1.config` be placed in the application source bundle?
question.pt: Onde an Elastic Beanstalk configuration arquivo named `healthcheckur1.config` be placed em o application source bundle?

en: In the `root` of the application.
In the `bin` folder.
In `healthcheckur1.config.ebextension` under `root`.
In the `.ebextensions` folder.
pt: In o `root` of o application.
In o `bin` folder.
In `healthcheckur1.config.ebextension` under `root`.
In o `.ebextensions` folder.

correct.D: Correct. Elastic Beanstalk `.config` files belong in the `.ebextensions` directory at the root of the source bundle. Concept assessed: Beanstalk .ebextensions. Exam trap: wrong folder/extension. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/ebextensions.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. Using the root user violates least privilege. Concept assessed: Beanstalk .ebextensions. Exam trap: wrong folder/extension.

wrong.C: Incorrect. Using the root user violates least privilege. Concept assessed: Beanstalk .ebextensions. Exam trap: wrong folder/extension.

correct.D: Correta. Arquivos `.config` do Elastic Beanstalk ficam no diretório `.ebextensions` na raiz do source bundle. Conceito avaliado: Beanstalk .ebextensions. Armadilha da prova: wrong folder/extension. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/elasticbeanstalk/latest/dg/ebextensions.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Usar root viola least privilege. Conceito avaliado: Beanstalk .ebextensions. Armadilha da prova: wrong folder/extension.

question.en: A Developer has implemented a Lambda function that needs to add new customers to an RDS database that is expected to run hundreds of times per hour. The Lambda function is configured to use 512MB of RAM and is based on the following pseudo code. After testing the Lambda function, the Developer notices that the Lambda execution time is much longer than expected. What should the Developer do to improve performance?
question.pt: Um Developer implementou uma função Lambda que precisa adicionar novos clientes a um banco de dados RDS e que deve ser executada centenas de vezes por hora. A função Lambda está configurada para usar 512 MB de RAM e é baseada no seguinte pseudocódigo. Após testar a função Lambda, o Developer percebe que o tempo de execução do Lambda é muito maior do que o esperado. O que o Developer deve fazer para melhorar o desempenho?

en: Increase the amount of RAM allocated to the Lambda function, which will increase the number of threads the Lambda can use.
Increase the size of the RDS database to allow for an increased number of database connections each hour.
Move the database connection and close statement out of the handler. Place the connection in the global space.
Replace RDS wit Amazon DynamoDB to implement control over the number of writes per second.
pt: Aumentar a quantidade de RAM alocada para a função Lambda, o que aumentará o número de threads que o Lambda pode usar.
Aumentar o tamanho do banco de dados RDS para permitir um maior número de conexões de banco de dados por hora.
Mover a instrução de conexão e fechamento do banco de dados para fora do handler. Colocar a conexão no espaço global.
Substituir o RDS pelo Amazon DynamoDB para implementar controle sobre o número de gravações por segundo.

correct.C: Correct. Opening/closing database connections inside the handler adds latency; placing the connection in global scope allows reuse in warm Lambda environments. Concept assessed: Lambda connection reuse. Exam trap: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-memory.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. More memory does not fix repeated DB connection creation. Concept assessed: Lambda connection reuse. Exam trap: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection.
wrong.B: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: Lambda connection reuse. Exam trap: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection.
wrong.D: Incorrect. RDS is durable relational storage, not the lowest-latency shared memory store. Concept assessed: Lambda connection reuse. Exam trap: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection.

correct.C: Correta. Abrir/fechar conexão no handler adiciona latência; colocar conexão no escopo global permite reuso em ambientes Lambda aquecidos. Conceito avaliado: Lambda connection reuse. Armadilha da prova: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-memory.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Mais memória não corrige criação repetida de conexão. Conceito avaliado: Lambda connection reuse. Armadilha da prova: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection.
wrong.B: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: Lambda connection reuse. Armadilha da prova: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection.
wrong.D: Incorreta. RDS é relacional durável, não memória compartilhada de menor latência. Conceito avaliado: Lambda connection reuse. Armadilha da prova: scaling memory/DB instead of reusing connection.

question.en: A static website is hosted in an Amazon S3 bucket. Several HTML pages on the site use JavaScript to download images from another Amazon S3 bucket. These images are not displayed when users browse the site. What is the possible cause for the issue?
question.pt: Um site estático está hospedado em um bucket Amazon S3. Várias páginas HTML no site usam JavaScript para baixar imagens de outro bucket Amazon S3. Essas imagens não são exibidas quando os usuários navegam pelo site. Qual é a possível causa do problema?

en: The referenced Amazon S3 bucket is in another region.
The images must be stored in the same Amazon S3 bucket.
Port 80 must be opened on the security group in which the Amazon S3 bucket is located.
Cross Origin Resource Sharing must be enabled on the Amazon S3 bucket.
pt: O bucket Amazon S3 referenciado está em outra região.
As imagens devem ser armazenadas no mesmo bucket Amazon S3.
A porta 80 deve ser aberta no security group em que o bucket Amazon S3 está localizado.
O Cross Origin Resource Sharing deve ser habilitado no bucket Amazon S3.

correct.D: Correct. Browser JavaScript requests from one S3 bucket origin to another are cross-origin and require CORS on the target bucket. Concept assessed: S3 CORS. Exam trap: thinking same bucket/security group/region is required. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/cors.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (S3 CORS). The correct mechanism is required because browser JavaScript requests from one S3 bucket origin to another are cross-origin and require CORS on the target bucket. Concept assessed: S3 CORS. Exam trap: thinking same bucket/security group/region is required.
wrong.B: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (S3 CORS). The correct mechanism is required because browser JavaScript requests from one S3 bucket origin to another are cross-origin and require CORS on the target bucket. Concept assessed: S3 CORS. Exam trap: thinking same bucket/security group/region is required.
wrong.C: Incorrect. S3 buckets do not use EC2 security groups. Concept assessed: S3 CORS. Exam trap: thinking same bucket/security group/region is required.

correct.D: Correta. Requisições JavaScript de um bucket S3 para outro são cross-origin e exigem CORS no bucket de destino. Conceito avaliado: S3 CORS. Armadilha da prova: thinking same bucket/security group/region is required. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/cors.html

wrong.A: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (S3 CORS). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque requisições JavaScript de um bucket S3 para outro são cross-origin e exigem CORS no bucket de destino. Conceito avaliado: S3 CORS. Armadilha da prova: thinking same bucket/security group/region is required.
wrong.B: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (S3 CORS). O mecanismo correto é necessário porque requisições JavaScript de um bucket S3 para outro são cross-origin e exigem CORS no bucket de destino. Conceito avaliado: S3 CORS. Armadilha da prova: thinking same bucket/security group/region is required.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Buckets S3 não usam security groups EC2. Conceito avaliado: S3 CORS. Armadilha da prova: thinking same bucket/security group/region is required.

question.en: Amazon S3 has the following structure: `S3://BUCKET/FOLDERNAME/FILENAME.zip`. Which S3 best practice would optimize performance with thousands of PUT request each second to a single bucket?
question.pt: O Amazon S3 possui a estrutura `S3://BUCKET/FOLDERNAME/FILENAME.zip`. Qual boa prática otimizaria performance com milhares de requisições PUT por segundo para um único bucket?

en: Prefix folder names with user id; for example, `s3://BUCKET/2013-FOLDERNAME/FILENAME.zip`.
Prefix file names with timestamps; for example, `s3://BUCKET/FOLDERNAME/2013-26-05-15-00-00-FILENAME.zip`.
Prefix file names with random hex hashes; for example, `s3://BUCKET/FOLDERNAME/23a6-FILENAME.zip`.
Prefix folder names with random hex hashes; for example, `s3://BUCKET/23a6-FOLDERNAME/FILENAME.zip`.
pt: Prefix folder names com user id; para example, `s3://BUCKET/2013-FOLDERNAME/FILENAME.zip`.
Prefix arquivo names com timestamps; para example, `s3://BUCKET/FOLDERNAME/2013-26-05-15-00-00-FILENAME.zip`.
Prefix arquivo names com random hex hashes; para example, `s3://BUCKET/FOLDERNAME/23a6-FILENAME.zip`.
Prefix folder names com random hex hashes; para example, `s3://BUCKET/23a6-FOLDERNAME/FILENAME.zip`.

correct.D: Correct. Historical S3 guidance recommended randomizing the leading key prefix, such as the first folder-like component, to spread high PUT rates across partitions. Concept assessed: S3 key prefix performance. Exam trap: randomizing timestamps or later filename parts. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/optimizing-performance.html

wrong.A: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (S3 key prefix performance). The correct mechanism is required because historical S3 guidance recommended randomizing the leading key prefix, such as the first folder-like component, to spread high PUT rates across partitions. Concept assessed: S3 key prefix performance. Exam trap: randomizing timestamps or later filename parts.
wrong.B: Incorrect. Timestamps can concentrate writes under predictable prefixes. Concept assessed: S3 key prefix performance. Exam trap: randomizing timestamps or later filename parts.
wrong.C: Incorrect. This option does not address the tested behavior (S3 key prefix performance). The correct mechanism is required because historical S3 guidance recommended randomizing the leading key prefix, such as the first folder-like component, to spread high PUT rates across partitions. Concept assessed: S3 key prefix performance. Exam trap: randomizing timestamps or later filename parts.

correct.D: Correta. A orientação histórica do S3 recomendava randomizar o prefixo inicial da key, como o primeiro componente tipo pasta, para distribuir altas taxas de PUT entre partições. wrong.B: Incorreta. Timestamps podem concentrar gravações em prefixes previsíveis. Conceito avaliado: S3 key prefix performance. Armadilha da prova: randomizing timestamps or later filename parts.
wrong.C: Incorreta. Esta alternativa não trata o comportamento avaliado (S3 key prefix performance).
